⭐ North Stars

Philosophy & Strategy — Risk AOP FY27

What we're optimizing for and why
AOP FY27 Strategic Intent
AOP FY27 focuses on unlocking growth from the existing funnel, strengthening risk calibration through AI-enabled underwriting, process optimisation and risk monitoring — while creating new growth through new products, policy programs and eliminating worse cohorts.
Lead-Driven Growth
More leads → more disbursals
→
Efficiency-Driven Growth
Right risk at right price = unlock

Portfolio Quality — Improving Trajectory

GNPA & NNPA Line · Jul '25 – Jan '26
GNPA % NNPA %
GNPA Jul→Jan
2.55% → 1.35%
▼ 47% improvement
NNPA Jul→Jan
0.93% → 0.46%
▼ 51% improvement
Better Portfolio Mix
Higher AB share driving portfolio quality improvement
Better Risk Segregation
Risk Model and Policy improvements creating cleaner cohorts
Stronger Collections
Better own-book collections leading to lower roll rates
Legacy Book Run-off
High-risk legacy portfolio running off improving own-book metrics

Cost of Default (COD) by Channel

Based on Apr '24–Aug '25 vintages with minimum 3M performance
Channel COD
C2C2.71%
DCF3.99%
D2C4.04%
LAC3.26%
BT/TopUp3.13%
C2C is the cleanest channel on COD. DCF and D2C need better credit filtering.

6 Strategic Levers — FY27

Each lever has a target outcome and key initiatives
Strategic Lever
Key Initiatives
Expected Outcome
🎯
Funnel Optimization / Swap-ins
Stage-wise funnel fixes from ToF through approval. Experian Swap-in, Pmaxx swap-in in C2C, product-wise leakage correction.
Experian Model · Pmaxx C2C swap-in · Stage-wise drop-off correction · Product funnel benchmarking
~10% Approval Uplift
🚀
Product Innovation
Trade Advance, Balloon Repayment, Internal Top-Up — structured productization of new lending constructs within risk guardrails.
Trade Advance Policy & BRE · Balloon Repayment Policy · Internal Top-Up finalization · RC Limit system
+15–20% DCF Growth
+5–7% Approval Uplift
+2.5–4% MoM from TopUp
⚙️
BOF Automation
FCU automation, AI TVR, BRE optimization, 100% credit decisioning on Saarthi — compress decision cycle time end-to-end.
AI TVR calling · Prefilled CAM · FI trigger automation · Dedupe automation · 100% Saarthi decisioning · BRE AI optimization
-20–30% TAT
-40% Manual Intervention
🧠
Leveraging Risk DS Models
Imputed Income Model usage & scale-up, V7 model development & migration in Q2, behavioral propensity scoring for PA.
Imputed Income scale-up · V7 model (Q2 migration) · Behavioral risk + propensity model · Employer categorization via GenAI
Better Risk Differentiation + PA Volumes
🛡️
Risk Intelligence Framework
EWS, Dealer Risk Scoring, Employer categorization via GenAI, Portfolio & TTD monitoring dashboard — see problems before they cost money.
EWS Framework · Mother Risk Metrics · Tableau automation · Dealer Risk Model · Employer Cat Master · Portfolio TTD dashboard
-10–15% Bad Rate Drift
📋
Governance & Controls
LOS DLA & Deviation validations, Internal Policy Approval Framework, BRE Control Charts — institutional-grade accountability at every decision point.
Internal Policy Approval Matrix · LOS Audit Log · BRE Control Chart · Soft rejection for FCU · DLA & Deviation in Saarthi
Faster Approvals
Zero Non-Compliance

Credit Benchmarking — Loans24 vs Peers

Approval Rate · TAT · Productivity
Lender SP Approval LAC Approval BT Approval TAT Productivity (cases/agent/day)

Bajaj Finance68%36%—50 min–1 hr6
Piramal Finance66%71%70%1.2 hrs12
AU Finance75%70%—5 hrs11

Highest SP approval rate (80% vs 55–75% peers)
Gap to Close
LAC approval 27% vs 36–71% peers
Productivity
10–12/day at par with peers; target 17

Metric C2C DCF D2C ReFi-LAC ReFi-BT Total
Total Leads44,0187,59013,0326,0544,18574,879

Team Design — FY27

Hiring & Capacity Planning
Risk & Policy Team
2 positions joining in March — will stabilise the team. Dedicated Research, Benchmarking & Market Intelligence SPOC to be established in FY27.
Credit & FCU Team
2 key positions joining in March — bandwidth tightening normalises Apr onwards. Underwriting agent manpower planning per business projections with focus on productivity optimization over headcount growth.

Themes — Deep Dive

5 Themes · All initiatives with full objectives, approach & metrics
⚡
Theme 1 — TAT Improvement & Productivity Enhancement
Goal: 40 min → 25 min per case · 12 → 17 cases/agent/day · Cost saving ~₹3.5L
P0P1
T1.1
AI TVR and Prefilled CAM
P0
TVR calling and collection of basic info through AI Agent. Data auto-captured in Saarthi on completion.

Agent Productivity: From 12 to 17 cases/day (37.5% unlock).
Cost saving ~₹3.5L — 30 agents at present, salary saving for 11 agents at avg ₹32K.
TAT breakdown: 5 min CIBIL & banking summary · 3 min AI TVR (50% cases sorted) · 2 min CRM & TVR connectivity · 2 min FI initiation · 3 min TVR workstation revamp (40+ clicks today → streamlined).
How: Conversion of speech to text connected to Saarthi data pipeline. AI agent conducts TVR, captures structured data, auto-populates CAM fields.
Metric / Outcome: From 40 min → 25 min per case. Agent throughput: 12 → 17 cases/day.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
T1.2
100% Credit Decisioning on Saarthi
P0
Transition all credit decisioning to Saarthi by discontinuing email-based decisioning. Improves process efficiency, reduces operational risk, enhances transparency, and strengthens compliance and audit controls.
How: CAM Enhancement · Policy coding on Sentinel for Refi and BT · Separate table to capture BT details
Metric / Outcome: 100% cases decided in Saarthi. Zero email-based decisions.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
T1.3
TVR Optimisation
P1
Optimise credit agent workflow through AI summaries and data points helping in reduction of TAT per agent and enhancing overall productivity.
How: Enhancement of CIBIL/Loan Obligation Summary for eligibility calculation · Banking intelligence through AI with summary · Eliminating or solving for Banking Sendback (Salaried IP)
Metric / Outcome: Reduction in agent handling time per TVR case.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
T1.4
CRM Automation
P1
Auto-trigger CRM wherever TVR is pending, primary banking is not provided by customer, or co-app addition is required.
How: Push CRMs through Clevertap based on customer preferred slot to eliminate multiple calls.
Metric / Outcome: Reduction in manual CRM outreach. Higher TVR completion rate.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
T1.5
FI Trigger Automation & Vendor Integration
P1
Eliminate email-based FI initiation by enabling 100% automated FI triggers and vendor integration directly within Saarthi. Eliminates multiple Google Sheets and manual form-fill by Credit and FCU.
How: Auto-initiation of FI through Saarthi basis rules post approval · Vendor report received directly in Saarthi · Eliminates Google Form fill by Credit and FCU
Metric / Outcome: 100% automated FI triggers. Zero email-based FI initiation.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
T1.6
Co-App TVR Calling Through Saarthi + Incoming Call Facility
P1
Enable TVR calling through Saarthi for cases where co-applicant is roped into the deal, maintaining full call records in system.
How: Enabling calling feature in Saarthi for co-applicants similar to existing primary applicant calling capability.
Metric / Outcome: Full call records in system for co-app TVR. Consistent audit trail.
Owner: Tushal
🚀
Theme 2 — Policy Innovation & Business Enablement
New products within risk guardrails · 10–20% approval uplift potential · ₹-incremental monthly disbursals
P0
T2.1
Pmaxx / Subprime & Reject Cohort Swap-in
P0
Segmented underwriting through reject inferencing. Pmaxx model swap-in specifically for C2C channel to recover approvals from customers currently rejected by standard policy.
How: Reject inferencing using Experian Model · Pmaxx Swap-in test in C2C · Controlled A/B test to validate credit performance
Metric / Outcome: 10%+ Approval uplift from rejected cohort re-evaluation.
Owner: Tushar/Laxmi
T2.2
Trade Advance (TA) Structured Policy, SOP & BRE
P0
Enable Trade Advance lending with a structured policy and SOP framework embedded in LOS/BRE. DCF business growth target: 15–20% from TA enablement.
How: Draft end-to-end TA policy · Align with Ops, Risk, Tech teams · Embed rules in LOS/BRE · Create monitoring dashboards for TA portfolio performance
Metric / Outcome: 15–20% DCF business growth via Trade Advance.
Owner: Priyanka/Sandeep
T2.3
Balloon Repayment Policy & BRE
P0
Enable balloon repayment structure with 5–7% approval uplift potential. Affordability-calibrated risk — balloon structure helps customers with near-eligibility profiles.
How: Draft end-to-end policy covering eligible customers and balloon repayment rules · L1 discussion done with Bajaj to align on existing framework · BRE rules for balloon repayment structuring
Metric / Outcome: 5–7% approval uplift. Affordability-calibrated risk improvement.
Owner: Priyanka/Sandeep
T2.4
Internal Top-Up Offering Policy Finalization
P0
Enable internal top-up for existing customers as a new product. 2.5–4% monthly business growth with low CoC. PA base creation and performance monitoring in place.
How: Policy formulation covering eligibility, LTV rules, and risk guardrails · Periodic PA base creation from existing borrowers · Maintain dashboard for close performance monitoring
Metric / Outcome: 2.5–4% monthly business growth. Low cost of capital.
Owner: Sandeep
T2.5
RC Limit Productisation
P0
System-based tracking and control mechanism for RC limits. 140 dealers onboarded with ~₹40 Cr RC limit. Enable structured RC limit policy and better performance review.
How: System-based tracking and control mechanism implementation end-to-end · Replace manual tracking with LOS/dashboard integration
Metric / Outcome: Structured RC limit ecosystem. Real-time dealer RC tracking.
Owner: Sandeep/Achin
T2.6
AI Enabled Soft Decisioning
P1
20–30% reduction in manual underwriting time. Improved approval rate in borderline cases. Better decline reason explainability. Reduction in early bounce (first 2–3 EMI) and Approved-Bad population.
How: Bureau and AA reasoning co-pilots providing: 1) Credit behavior summary · 2) Reason for score movement · 3) Intent vs liquidity risk classification · 4) Suggested action (approve/cap/decline) · 5) Classification of AA Data using pretrained LLM for salary consistency detection
Metric / Outcome: 20–30% reduction in manual underwriting time. Reduced early bounce.
Owner: Achin/Laxmi/Tushal
T2.7
Deviation & Delegation of Authority (DLA) Coded in Saarthi
P1
Enable auto-routing of cases per the deviation/delegation matrix, eliminating manual escalation. Full DLA matrix embedded in LOS.
How: Full deviation and delegation matrix coded in Saarthi with auto-routing logic · Case automatically escalates to correct authority level
Metric / Outcome: Zero manual escalation. Faster approval TAT via auto-routing.
Owner: Achin/Tushal/Sandeep
T2.8
Dealer Risk Scoring Model
P1
Enhanced dealer risk monitoring and dealer swap-out based on actual performance data. Reduce exposure to underperforming dealer channels.
How: Build dealer dataset with performance history · ML risk scoring model · Monthly/quarterly monitoring dashboard · Swap-out triggers based on risk score thresholds
Metric / Outcome: -20% high-risk dealer exposure. Proactive swap-out before NPA impact.
Owner: RK/Shobhit
📊
Theme 3 — Risk Monitoring, BRE & Process Optimisation
Real-time portfolio visibility · Rule integrity · 30%+ faster risk detection
P0P1
T3.1
Tableau – Monthly Risk Deck Automation
P0
Faster turnaround and lower data error in monthly risk reporting. Replace manual data pulls with automated, scheduled Tableau dashboards.
How: Centralized risk mart · Automated ETL pipeline · Scheduled Tableau refresh · Validation checks on data quality
Metric / Outcome: Zero manual risk deck creation. 100% automated monthly refresh.
Owner: Tushar/Aman
T3.2
Mother Risk Metrics & EWS Framework
P0
10–15% risk saving via early detection. 30%+ faster risk detection speed. Clear risk metrics relevant for AOP monitoring. Framework to get triggers for good and bad cohorts on TTD (Time to Default) metrics.
How: Define comprehensive risk metrics relevant for AOP monitoring · Build EWS triggers for good and bad cohorts on TTD · Framework covers: vintage performance, roll rates, 0+/30+/90+ DPD trends · Automated triggers for early intervention
Metric / Outcome: 10–15% risk saving. 30%+ faster early risk detection. EWS live for all cohorts.
Owner: Devendra
T3.3
BRE Optimization via AI
P0
Optimize BRE flow, reduce API size and latency, improve codification efficiency using AI. Eliminate redundant nodes and unnecessary API calls.
How: AI for BRE flow redesign · Identify and eliminate redundant nodes · Optimize rule sequencing for performance · Monitor latency KPIs pre and post optimization
Metric / Outcome: Improved BRE latency. Reduced API call size. Faster rule execution.
Owner: Ria/Sandeep
T3.4
Live Rejection Rule Tracker
P0
Real-time tracking of rule-wise rejections to quickly identify anomalies. Immediately surface when a BRE rule is rejecting unexpectedly high or low volumes.
How: Create rule performance mart · Connect LOS + BRE data · Live dashboard with anomaly detection and alerting · Triggers when any rule deviation exceeds threshold
Metric / Outcome: Live rule anomaly detection. Immediate alert on unexpected rejection spikes.
Owner: Sandeep/Aman
T3.5
AI-enabled Portfolio Risk & TTD Trend Monitoring Dashboard
P1
Live portfolio review and faster decision making with quick swap in/out capabilities. AI-driven anomaly detection for negative performance indicators across segments and policies.
How: Centralized live risk dashboard · AI for anomaly detection · Automated trend analysis · Triggers for negative performance indicators · Segment and policy-level drill-downs
Metric / Outcome: Live portfolio visibility. Faster swap-in/out decisions. Automated anomaly alerts.
Owner: Tushar/Abhishek S
T3.6
Auto Deviation, DLA & Critical Flag Engine
P1
Eliminate missed critical bureau/soft derog signals. Standardize deviation capture. Auto-flag cases that require escalation before they reach wrong approval authority.
How: Integrate bureau parser with LOS · Rule-trigger alerts for critical flags · BRE auto-flagging for DLA deviations · Standardized deviation capture across all products
Metric / Outcome: Zero missed critical flags. Standardized deviation capture. Auto-routing accuracy.
Owner: Achin/Tushal/Sandeep
T3.7
Employer Categorization Master
P1
10–15% risk saving by applying extra checks on Non-Cat companies. GenAI-based employer classification providing risk-differentiated underwriting.
How: Centralized employer master with risk tagging (Cat A/B/C/D + Govt/Schools/Hospitals) · Link employer category to funding caps in BRE · Periodic employer risk review and updates · FCU automation improves data capture making final model more robust
Metric / Outcome: 10–15% risk saving. Differentiated policy for high-risk employer segments.
Owner: Sandeep/Abhishek S
T3.8
BRE Control Chart
P1
Improved rule accuracy, reduced policy leakage, early anomaly detection in BRE rule performance.
How: Re-code all BRE policy rules in parallel (rule-wise validation layer) · Compare BRE outputs vs validation logic to identify mismatches · Build live dashboard to track rule performance and failure rates · Set up daily automated triggers/alerts for any deviation or anomaly
Metric / Outcome: 100% BRE rule validation coverage. Zero policy leakage from miscoded rules.
Owner: Kashish/Sandeep
🔒
Theme 4 — Fraud Prevention & FCU Automation
AI-first FCU · Unstructured doc detection · AML automation · Target: 90% FCU checks automated
P0
T4.1
Auto Fraud Detection in Unstructured Documents
P0
Automate FCU checks and data/information validations via DS logic and AI. Target documents: Utility Bill, WiFi Bill, E-Rent Agreements, Mobile Postpaid Bill.
How: FCU Automation in GC_BL cases — data triangulation across multiple data points · API integrations for document authenticity verification · DS models for pattern detection in unstructured docs
Metric / Outcome: 90%+ FCU document checks automated. Fraud detection without manual review.
Owner: Achin/Prerna/Tushal
T4.2
Automation of FCU Process
P0
Full automation of FCU check workflows to reduce manual touchpoints across all document types and verification steps.
How: API integrations for FCU check automation · Automated workflows replacing manual FCU agent steps · Exception handling for edge cases requiring human review
Metric / Outcome: Zero manual FCU for standard cases. Human review reserved for exceptions only.
Owner: Achin/Prerna/Tushal
T4.3
External Tools — ZIGRAM, TartanHQ, HyperVerge
P0
Automate AML checks, FI verification, and FCU workflows via best-in-class external platforms. Reduce dependency on manual vendor coordination.
How: ZIGRAM for AML check automation · TartanHQ for FI and VPD (Vehicle Physical Document) verification · HyperVerge for FCU document automation · POC first, then production integration
Metric / Outcome: AML checks automated. FI/VPD via AI. FCU via API integration.
Owner: Achin/Prerna/Tushal
T4.4
Dedupe Automation
P0
Automate deduplication checks at lead, asset, and fraud levels. Proactively detect duplicate cases, reduce risk exposure, and improve process efficiency.
How: Automated dedup at 3 levels: 1) Lead dedup (same customer multiple logins) · 2) Asset dedup (same vehicle multiple applications) · 3) Fraud dedup (ring patterns) · Integrated in LOS flow
Metric / Outcome: 100% dedup checks automated at all 3 levels. Ring fraud detection live.
Owner: Sandeep/Achin/Tushal
T4.5
Soft Rejection for FCU in Saarthi
P0
FCU can now reject cases identified as fraud or document manipulation directly in Saarthi with captured reasons. Strengthens dedupe framework with system-recorded rejections.
How: Implement soft rejection module in Saarthi for FCU team · Mandatory reason capture for all FCU rejections · Rejection data feeds into dedupe and fraud pattern models
Metric / Outcome: 100% FCU rejections in system with reasons. Fraud pattern data improves over time.
Owner: Sandeep/Tushal
T4.6
VPD with AI
P1
AI-based Vehicle Physical Document verification via POC with TartanHQ. Reduce manual VPD process time and improve accuracy.
How: POC with TartanHQ for FI/VPD verification via AI · Evaluate accuracy vs manual process · Define production rollout plan based on POC results
Metric / Outcome: AI-based VPD verification live. Reduced manual FI/VPD process time.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
📋
Theme 5 — Governance, Compliance & Control
Full audit trail · DLA matrix · BRE control · Process optimisation
P0P1
T5.1
Internal Policy Approval Framework
P0
Establish structured delegation matrix defining approval authority levels to strengthen internal controls. Streamline policy/product/model approvals and ensure decisioning at the correct authority level with reduced TAT.
How: Structure and implement clear approval matrix framework · Map all policy types to correct authority levels · Digitize approval workflow · Monitor approval TAT by type
Metric / Outcome: Zero non-compliant approvals. Structured DLA matrix live.
Owner: Sandeep Agarwal
T5.2
Audit Trail / Workflow Change Visibility (LOS Field Change Audit Log Engine)
P1
Ensure complete traceability of all LOS data changes by capturing the change logs. Enable visibility of changes done post-FCU acceptance of documents. Helpful for hindsighting and audit. E.g., visibility of changes done post-FCU document acceptance; reasons for case re-look.
How: Maintaining synchronized change logs in both Saarthi LOS and database · Capture all field-level modifications with complete traceability and user mapping · Activity log enhancement for modifications in relook cases
Metric / Outcome: 100% field-level change traceability. Full audit log for all LOS modifications.
Owner: Sandeep/Tushal/Achin
T5.3
Co-Applicant Flow Improvement
P1
Filter ineligible age profiles at sourcing stage by enforcing earning co-applicant rule. Reduce operational waste from processing age-ineligible cases through full credit cycle.
How: Age logic implementation at ToF — auto-trigger for co-app addition when primary applicant age is outside eligible range · CRM communication to customer about co-app requirement
Metric / Outcome: Reduced age-ineligible processing. Better approval efficiency from ToF.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
T5.4
Multiple Perfios for Same Banking Account
P1
Eliminate duplication of Perfios reports by enabling one complete report per banking account, even if the bank statement is uploaded multiple times.
How: Deduplication logic for Perfios reports at account level · One report generated per unique account regardless of upload count
Metric / Outcome: Zero duplicate Perfios reports. Cost reduction in Perfios API calls.
Owner: Achin/Tushal

Detailed Project List

All 34 Projects · Full Objectives, Approach & Owners
⚡ T1 TAT & Productivity
01
AI TVR and Prefilled CAM
P0
Productivity unlock by 37.5% per agent. TAT: 40 min → 25 min. Agent throughput: 12 → 17 cases/day. Cost saving ~₹3.5L (11 agents freed at avg ₹32K/month).
How: Speech-to-text + Saarthi pipeline. 5 min CIBIL summary + 3 min AI TVR + 2 min CRM + 2 min FI initiation + 3 min workstation revamp.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
02
100% Credit Decisioning on Saarthi
P0
Discontinue email-based decisioning. Improve transparency, reduce operational risk, strengthen audit controls.
How: CAM Enhancement + Sentinel policy coding for Refi/BT + BT-specific capture table.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
03
TVR Optimisation
P1
Reduce agent handling time via AI summaries for CIBIL, banking, and loan obligations.
How: CIBIL/Loan Obligation Summary enhancement + Banking intelligence via AI + Eliminate Banking Sendback (Salaried IP).
Owner: Achin/Tushal
04
CRM Automation
P1
Auto-trigger CRMs for TVR pending, missing primary banking, or co-app required.
How: Clevertap integration based on customer preferred slot.
Owner: Achin/Tushal
05
FI Trigger Automation & Vendor Integration
P1
100% automated FI triggers. Eliminate Google Sheets and email-based FI initiation entirely.
How: Auto-initiation post approval in Saarthi. Vendor report delivered in Saarthi directly.
Owner: Achin/Tushal

Co-App TVR Calling + Incoming Call
P1
Call records in Saarthi for co-applicant TVR. Consistent audit trail.

Owner: Tushal

🧭 Philosophy
🌐 Ecosystem
📊 FY26 Performance
💰 Wallet Share
🎯 FY27 Priorities
⭐ North Stars
VISION · 3-YEAR AMBITION
10× Growth — 1,500 to 15,000 Disbursals
From a lending utility to the most trusted, fastest, and most profitable financing partner in the used car ecosystem — capturing India's ₹8,500 Cr/month NBFC used car lending opportunity.
10×
Volume Target
Monthly Disbursals
3
Year Horizon
FY25 → FY28
~3%
Target ROA
vs 2.0% Peer Avg
-96%
TAT Reduction
18 hrs → 0.7 hrs
WHY 10× IS ACHIEVABLE
16.6%
Ecosystem penetration today (450 of 2,946 stockin dealers active)
83.4%
Stockin dealers not yet receiving DCF disbursals
-96%
TAT reduction already achieved (18 hrs → 0.7 hrs)
3×
Own book growth in 11 months as TAT compressed

Built on Two Beliefs

⚡
BELIEF 1 · HOW WE WIN
Speed & Transparency
The fastest approval-to-disbursal TAT in the segment (18 hrs → under 1 hr) is not a feature — it is our primary growth engine. Every 1-hour improvement in TAT directly expands our A/B share and own book. Dealers and customers get honest, instant, no-black-box answers.
🏗️
BELIEF 2 · HOW WE SCALE
Ecosystem Moat
18,000+ IMPS dealers, 2,900+ active stockin partners — no competitor can replicate this data and distribution depth. DCF's structural edge is not just a product; it is the privileged position inside the largest organised used car network in India. This moat compounds with every new dealer activated.

Executed Through Four Pillars

PILLAR 01
Offering at Speed
18 hrs → <1 hr P50 TAT
Sub-hour approvals, same-day disbursals on clean files. Instant, policy-driven decisions at scale. Speed parity with — and increasingly ahead of — Bajaj & IDFC.
PILLAR 02
Predictability
Consistent. No surprises.
Clear, stable credit criteria. Dealers can forecast which cases will clear before they submit. Predictability builds trust, trust drives dealer channel stickiness and repeat flow.
PILLAR 03
Engagement
DCF as the default first call
On-ground RMs, proactive dealer communication, co-created GTM programs. We embed ourselves in how dealers think and operate — not just where they send loan applications.
PILLAR 04
Commercial Success for Partner
Full financial stack for dealers
Subvention, Trade Advance, stocking finance, referral income. When dealer economics improve because of DCF, our book scales. Aligned incentives — not just a loan product.

Dealer Data — Transaction ↔ DCF

Path to scale is activation, not acquisition
DCF doesn't need to acquire new dealers to grow 10x. Cars24 ecosystem already has 3000 active / 8000 bidding dealers.
Path to scale is activation, not acquisition.
80%+
of stockin dealers not yet active with DCF

IMPS Dealers (same base)
9% engage DCF

Key Takeaway

FY26 — Progress on Key Pillars

Pillar 01 · Offering at Speed 02 · Predictability 03 · Engagement & CX 04 · Commercial Success
FY26 Ambition Instant Decisions · Lead to Approval <1hr 95%+ decision consistency with real-time pricing & approval transparency 90%+ Dealer Retention, led by ecosystem engagement & seamless disbursal experience 5%+ dealer payouts · Best-in-market ROI with faster payouts and differentiated rewards
FY26 Outcomes ✅ Inspection-less approvals
✅ 100% OV (no change post inspection, Hybrid pilot South)
✅ Banking-less share 50%+
✅ Self-serve Lead→Approval on Miles & Dealer App
✅ Tatkal (low LTV for low AQB) launched		 ✅ Real-time lead status + offer visibility on App
✅ Higher LTV + lower charges for good CX (A/B tested)
✅ Upfront visibility on Dealer App for Loans
✅ Free CIBIL Check launched on Dealer App
✅ Valuation consistency improved via evaluator changes		 ✅ Standardized RC Transfers
✅ RC Limits + Self RC empowerment
✅ DCF-linked Unnati program
✅ RC Transfer visibility on Dealer App
✅ Real-time commission release with disbursal		 ✅ Competitive monthly payouts
✅ Quarterly bonus + foreign trips
✅ Cashback / lower ROI for Unnati dealers
✅ Interest-free Trade Advance pilot launched
✅ Transparency: Disbursal, Commission, Holdback on App

🧭 Philosophy

🏦 Borrowing Mix

🚀 North Stars

Treasury Philosophy — FY27

3 pillars · Capital efficiency · Disciplined leverage · Diversified borrowing
Strategic Mandate
Fund the growth engine at the lowest sustainable cost — while protecting the balance sheet from leverage and ALM risk.
Pillar 1
Capital-Efficient Growth
Co-lending to fund 65% of disbursements in FY27 — own-book grows without proportional balance sheet expansion
Pillar 2
Leverage Discipline
3.5x hard ceiling on Debt/Equity. FY27 planned at 2.40x — comfortable 1.1x headroom buffer maintained at all times
Pillar 3
Diversified Borrowing
Priority-ranked instrument mix guided by cost, ALM fit, and operational bandwidth. Inter-Co → PTC → DA → TL hierarchy
Rating Ambition
A– by H1 FY27
Unlock NCD/ECB access and reduce CoF further. Currently rated BBB+/A– supported by parent uplift

Cashflow Overview — FY26 → FY28 (₹ Crore)

Item FY26 FY27 FY28
Loan Book (Own)₹1,560 Cr₹2,110 Cr₹2,835 Cr
Total Disbursement₹1,560 Cr₹3,100 Cr₹4,500 Cr
Borrowings (OS)₹1,009 Cr₹1,659 Cr₹2,529 Cr
Leverage (D/E)1.60x2.40x3.37x
Collections₹300 Cr₹550 Cr₹775 Cr
PAT₹25 Cr₹50 Cr₹75 Cr

FY26 Key Learnings

Borrowings scaled 2.5x (₹409 Cr → ₹1,009 Cr) · Zero ALM breaches · 4 critical learnings
L1
CoF Reduction via Repo Rate Cycle & Instrument Mix
90 bps reduction achieved in FY26 — driven by rate cycle benefit and prioritising Inter-Co & PTC. This informs the FY27 instrument priority matrix heavily.
L2
DA/PTC: Fast Conversion but High Operational Bandwidth
DA and PTC are our fastest instruments (<1 month close) but intensive on structuring bandwidth. FY27 capacity planned accordingly — PTC ₹250 Cr, DA ₹100 Cr.
L3
Term Loans Take 2–3 Months to Close; Must Pipeline Early
TLs are structural, not tactical. Pipeline management requires 2–3 months advance lead time. FY27 TL planned at ₹200 Cr requires engagement starting Q1.
L4
WCDL Capped at 25–30% of Overall Borrowings
WCDLs are ALM-negative — short tenor against long assets. Hard cap at 25-30%. FY27 plan: ₹50 Cr. Beyond this creates meaningful liability mismatch risk.

Disbursement Strategy — FY27 vs FY26

Metric FY26 FY27 FY28
Total Disbursement₹1,560 Cr₹3,100 Cr₹4,500 Cr
Own Book (100%)₹1,145 Cr₹600 Cr₹750 Cr
Co-lending (20% own)₹35 Cr₹500 Cr₹750 Cr
Co-lending Share of Total24%65%67%
Avg. COF9.40%≤9.50%≤9.50%

FY27 Strategic Objectives

01
Maintain COF ≤9.50%
Instrument mix optimisation + rate cycle benefit. Lender competition to drive spread compression.
FY26 CoF: 9.40% → Hold & improve
02
Rating Upgrade to A–
Target H1 FY27. Unlocks NCD/ECB access, reduces COF by 50–75 bps.
Currently BBB+/A– (supported)
03
Add 5+ New Lenders (21+ total)
Reduce concentration risk. Cap single lender at 15% of borrowings.
₹1,605 Cr pipeline across 21+ partners
04
Increase Avg. Tenor to 36 Months
Strengthen liability structure with longer-tenor funding to reduce ALM drag.
Current avg. tenor <30 months
05
Liquidity Buffer ≥3 Months
Forward disbursement cover via undrawn lines at all times.
Undrawn: ₹500 Cr in base case
06
Cap Single Lender at 15%
Reduce top-5 lender concentration from ~45% in FY26.
Concentration risk mitigation

Treasury prepared for 3 growth scenarios

Own Book Disbursal₹1,100 Cr₹1,250 Cr₹1,400 Cr
Gross Debt Required₹1,200 Cr₹1,300 Cr₹1,500 Cr
Peak Leverage2.3x2.5x2.7x
Term Loan₹450 Cr₹450 Cr₹500 Cr
DA/PTC₹600 Cr₹650 Cr₹700 Cr

Credit Rating Assessment — FY26 Actuals

Mapped against CARE/CRISIL/ICRA/Ind-Ra NBFC framework
Metric AAA AA A BBB CARS24 FY26 Band
CRAR>25%22–25%20–22%18–20%~38%✓ AAA
Debt / Equity<3x3–4x4–5x5–6x1.60x✓ AAA
Gross NPA<1%1–2%2–3%3–5%~1.2%~ AA
ROA>3%2.5–3%2–2.5%1–2%~2.2%~ A
Liquidity Coverage>1.5x1.3–1.5x1.2–1.3x1.0–1.2xC&CE ₹10 Cr~ BBB
AUM Size>₹1L Cr₹40k–1L Cr₹10k–40k Cr<₹10k Cr₹1,560 Cr~ BBB
Composite Rating — FY27 Target
Standalone: BBB+ to A– · With parent/governance uplift (+1 notch): A– (Supported). Primary drag is AUM scale (₹1,560 Cr vs ₹10k Cr A-threshold). FY27 trajectory: ₹2,110 Cr own + ₹4,300 Cr total AUM positions us firmly for upgrade.

🧭 Philosophy & Moats
🎯 9 Strategic Themes
🛠️ Projects
⭐ North Stars

CeX Philosophy

Designing a Scalable, Regulatory-Controlled Customer Operating System
Core Belief
"CeX is not just about solving issues — it's about how the customer feels during and after every interaction across the lending lifecycle."
⚡
Ease of Access
Self-serve, WhatsApp & app-first journeys
💬
Clarity
Transparent communication at every stage
⏱
Resolution Speed
FRT & TAT as measurable operating KPIs
🎯
Resolution Quality
Root cause fixes, not temporary patches
📋
Policy Transparency
Charges, NOC, RC — no hidden complexity. Customers must understand what they signed up for.
🤝
Empathy
Human-centred tone across every channel, even as AI handles more volume.

3 Competitive Moats — FY27

🤖
Moat 1 — AI-First Customer Operations
Scale without proportional cost · Target: 30–40% deflection from human agents
AI handles 55% low-intent customers (info queries) without agent intervention. VoiceBot & Email AI as primary contact layer. Automated NACH, NOC, LFC, SOA generation.
Why it matters: Tickets grew 70% while team stayed the same. AI is the only path to scale.
🎯
Moat 2 — Zero-Error Core Lending Operations
Eliminate systemic complaint drivers
NACH & EMI collection automation (Jan'26 glitch = 908 tickets alone). Straight-through refund processing. RC transfer real-time tracking & communication. CIBIL accuracy & holdback reconciliation.
Why it matters: Top 3 complaint drivers (RC delay, NACH, Holdback) = 73% of all tickets.
🔭
Moat 3 — Unified Customer Intelligence (360°)
Every agent = a customer expert
Zendesk + Pennant + Saarthi + Spaceport single view. Full interaction history: calls, email, WhatsApp by Loan ID. Tableau CeX analytics control tower. Eliminate G-Sheet dependency across teams.
Why it matters: DSAT rose to 70% despite TAT improvement — quality gap, not speed gap.

9 Strategic Themes for FY27

T1 · Frictionless Servicing
DIY & self-serve for top 5 request types
WhatsApp + App self-serve journeys. Reduce inbound contact rate from 4.5–7.6% to <3.5%.
T2 · Zero-Error Operations
NACH, payment posting & refund accuracy
Eliminate the systemic errors that generate 73% of tickets. Fix at source, not at resolution.
T3 · Full Audit Traceability
Unified interaction log per Loan ID
All calls, emails, WhatsApp consolidated by Loan ID. Audit-ready for RBI and internal review.
T4 · Customer 360 Platform
Zendesk + Pennant + Saarthi integration
Single view for every agent. Eliminate G-Sheet dependency. Reduce Repeat Contact Rate to <15%.
T5 · Event-Based Comms Engine
Journey-triggered auto-communications
CRM-triggered SMS/WhatsApp/Email at every lifecycle event. Target NPS D+60 from 18.7% → >40%.
T6 · Lower Cost-to-Serve
AI deflection · 25–30% cost reduction
Drive >40% of cases via AI/self-serve. Agent deflection rate >35%. Scale without headcount growth.
T7 · AI-Led Workflows
VoiceBot, Email AI, workflow automation
VoiceBot handling inbound & outbound. Email AI with auto-classify & first response. FRT <4hrs.
T8 · Cross-Functional Governance
Rapid Response Unit · RBI & SMT escalation
Zero maintainable RBI Ombudsman complaints. Grievance % of active loans from 1.22% → <0.5%.
T9 · CeX Control Tower
Real-time KPI dashboard & DSAT tracking
Tableau control tower with all KPIs live. Eliminate lagging G-Sheet reporting. Enable daily decision-making.

🧭 Philosophy & Moats
⚡ 5-Min Journey
🛠️ Projects
⭐ North Stars

Operations Philosophy — FY27

Faster · Safer · Smarter Operations · Automation Charter
Transformation Mandate
Profile-based, automated operations — where all risk checkpoints remain intact while systematically eliminating manual dependency at every process stage.
🎯
Moat 1 — Profile-Based Ops Framework
Not one-size-fits-all — differentiated journeys by risk profile
Differentiated operational journeys tailored to customer and risk profiles. Cohort 1 gets a 2-minute journey. Cohort 3 gets a full manual check. The system routes intelligently based on the risk signal.
⚡
Moat 2 — 5-Minute Disbursement Journey
Near real-time disbursement for eligible customers
Enable near real-time disbursement for Cohort 1 eligible customers through cohort-driven process design and automation. Target ≤5 min TAT for eligible cases. Automation penetration 80%+ for FTR cases.
🛡️
Moat 3 — Risk-Controlled Automation
Automate without compromising risk integrity
All risk checkpoints remain intact while systematically reducing manual dependency. 100% RCU check elimination for FTR-automated cases. Manual effort reduction 60%+ across fulfilment and RCU processes.

5-Minute Disbursement Journey — Cohort Model

3 cohorts · Differentiated by risk profile and automation readiness
Cohort 1
~2 Min
Fully Automated Journey
VKYCDIY (1.5 min)
VPDSkip
NACHDIY (0.5 min)
Reference CallSkip
Asset QCAutomated
RCUSkip / Auto
CHMSkip / Post
DisbursementAuto
Cohort 2
~20 Min
Semi-Automated Journey
VKYCDIY (1.5 min)
VPDDIY + OCR (2 min)
NACHDIY (0.5 min)
Reference CallAI-led (2 min)
Asset QCAutomated
RCUAutomated (8 min)
CHMPost Disbursement
DisbursementAuto (6 min)
Cohort 3
~3.5 Hrs
Manual / Agent Journey
VKYCPhysical KYC (5 min)
VPDManual Agent (5 min)
NACHPaper NACH (5 min)
Reference CallAgent (5 min)
Asset QCManual (15 min)
RCUManual (20 min)
CHMPre Disbursal (2 hrs)
DisbursementManual (15 min)

Area Project Automation Initiative Target Impact Moat
FulfilmentVKYC – DIY FlowCustomer self-service app with guided directions↓ Agent assistance · ↑ VKYC Penetration5-Min

FulfilmentNACH – DIY FlowCustomer app-based NACH with bank validation↓ Processing time · ↓ Agent dependency5-Min
FulfilmentVPD – OCR + Geo ValidationLat-long capture + OCR for SOL checkEliminate VPD forgery · Auto SOL flaggingRisk Auto
RCURule-Based RCU AutomationSystem-driven risk verification for FTR cases↓ Processing effort · Faster case closuresRisk Auto
RCUVKYC/NACH/Ref Call Audit SkipDIY-verified cases skip RCU re-checkEliminate redundant checks · ↓ Reallocation TATProfile
QCAsset QC & Challan AutomationVAHAN API + Regional Challan API via SpaceportEliminate manual RC & asset check · ↑ AccuracyRisk Auto
QCService History & Approval AutoSH check via API; auto-raise RTO/Credit subjectivitiesRealtime SH check · ↓ Manual approval follow-upsRisk Auto

Key Projects — CHM, Disbursal & RC Transfer

Area Project Automation Initiative Target Impact
CHMInstall/Uninstall AutomationMMI & Matchpoint API integration for CHM triggersAuto status + IMEI update · Auto invoicing & reconciliation
DisbursalRC Limit & Holdback AutomationSaarathi RC limit module + Spaceport holdback auto-calc↓ Disbursement TAT · Eliminate manual holdback checks
DisbursalDealer Blocking & NOC/FCLRule-based dealer blocking + OCR for NOC/Loan ClosureAuto block/unblock dealers · Accurate holdback calc
Post DisbursalAudit, Digital NOC & RTO KitAutomated hindsighting + Digital NOC + Saarathi file tracking↓ Manual doc verification · Faster NOC · Full file visibility
RC TransferSpaceport ↔ Saarathi IntegrationAutomate RTO costing, holdback tracking, end-to-end RC Transfer journey on LOS↓ Manual validation · Realtime RC Transfer & holdback visibility
RC TransferRTO Non-Operational MasterRed/Amber/Green RTO classification by TAT performanceProactive RTO governance · ↓ RC backlog · ≤90 day TAT
RC TransferCRM Triggers – Stage-Based CommsAutomated SMS/WhatsApp/Email on every RC milestone↓ Inbound queries · ↑ Customer transparency
RC TransferHypothecation Removal + DO ProcessAuto hypothecation via VAHAN API + Deemed Owner model pilot (Delhi, Mumbai, Karnataka)↓ Seller dependency · Faster RC transfer · ↓ Risk exposure

📊 Overview & Philosophy
🛠️ P0 Projects
✅ FY26 Delivered
👥 Team Structure
CFSPL FINANCE · ANNUAL OPERATING PLAN FY27
Theme — FinAI & Automation
4 PODs · 31 P0 Projects · IPO Readiness · MIS T+1 · Hard Close T+5

Our Philosophy & North Star

"Speed & Efficiency, P&L Enhancement, Accuracy & IPO Readiness with AI & Automation"
The Finance function is undergoing a fundamental transformation — moving from a reactive reporting unit to a proactive strategic nerve centre. Every initiative must compress cycle times, sharpen financial accuracy, and position CFSPL for a credible, audit-ready IPO journey — while AI becomes the connective tissue across all Finance workflows.
Business Finance
5% P&L
Enhancement of projected PBT for FY27
BizFin North Star
FP&A
MIS T+1
From current T+3
FP&A North Star
Controllership
Hard Close T+5
From current T+7
Controllership North Star
FinOps
First Time Right
Key enabler to FP&A & Controllership
FinOps North Star

Two Strategic Themes

🤝 Theme 1 — Finance as One Team
● Cross-functional leverage: Tap into diverse skills across BizFin, FP&A, Controllership and FinOps to solve problems collaboratively rather than in functional silos.
● Rotational roles: Design career paths that rotate talent across sub-functions to eliminate single-person dependencies and build institutional process memory.
● Horizontal org structure: Flatten hierarchy between all PODs — every function operates as an equal partner with a shared Finance scorecard.
● Merit & problem-solving culture: Reward those who identify and resolve friction. Reduce internal escalation overhead and gate-keeping dynamics.
🤖 Theme 2 — Think FinAI First
● Real-time AI Dashboard: Build AI-backed live financial dashboards surfacing anomalies and deep-dive insights without manual intervention.
● Faster decision-making: Leverage AI models for scenario planning, variance prediction and root-cause analysis — shifting Finance from look-back to forward-looking.
● Automation of non-value work: Every repetitive, manual, low-judgment task in FinOps, Controllership and FP&A must be automated — freeing the team for strategic work.
Biz Fin & FP&A
Lead: Pawan / Avnish · Forecasting, MIS, Data & Assurance
7 P0 Projects
Forecasting & Live P&L
Decision making on pricing, recovery strategy and cost optimization is reactive. Lack of real-time visibility into revenue, cost and profitability leads to delayed financial management.
May '26
MIS Automation, Dashboarding & Variance Analysis
Manual MIS preparation and fragmented reporting make it difficult to quickly identify performance gaps and variance drivers vs plan.
Jun '26
Data Repository
Data scattered across multiple systems leading to inconsistent reporting. Covers Disbursal, GL, HRMS, NPV P&L, Loan Books & Key Metrics.
May '26
Comprehensive Project Tracker
No centralized visibility of strategic initiatives, making it difficult to track progress, ownership, timelines and risks.
Jun '26
Benchmarking
Limited insight into industry best practices and peer performance, restricting identification of improvement opportunities.
May '26
Assurance — Revenue, Payments, Incentive & Payouts
Risk of financial leakages and inaccuracies in revenue recognition, partner payments and incentive payouts due to lack of validation controls.
Jun '26
NPV P&L True-up
Misalignment between projected NPV assumptions and actual outcomes resulting in inaccurate profitability visibility (Collection Cost & ECL).
Apr '26
Controllership
Lead: Yatin · Audit, IPO Readiness, Automation & Compliance
7 P0 Projects
Statutory Audit Fast Track
Accelerate and ensure smooth completion of statutory audit with robust documentation and zero-qualification outcomes.

Due Diligence — Pre-IPO Round & IPO Readiness
Support financial due diligence by providing reconciled historical data, audit trails and structured query resolution.

IPO Readiness (QoQ Financials, Segment Reporting, Accounting Positions)
Build IPO-ready financial disclosures including QoQ financials, segment reporting and standardized accounting positions.
May '26
Financial Reporting with AI
Implement AI-driven financial reporting and dashboards to enable real-time insights and proactive anomaly detection.
Jun '26
Balance Sheet & Working Capital Clean-up
Strengthen balance sheet data integrity through legacy cleanup, GL ownership and improved working capital transparency.
Mar '26
Cashflow Automation
Automate cashflow preparation and analysis to improve reporting speed and management visibility on liquidity.
May '26
MEC Timelines T+5
Achieve T+5 month-end close through process automation, defined ownership and stronger reconciliation controls.

FT & FinOps (Product)
Lead: Ankit · Disbursals, Refunds, NACH, Integrations & Collections
11 P0 Projects
CeX Charges Knock-off
Eliminates manual charge adjustments and ensures correct system-based charge knock-off, improving accuracy and reporting.
Mar '26
NACH Ad Hoc — DiGeo Integration
Enables system-driven NACH presentation and auto knock-off, removing manual intervention and improving CX, Fin-Ops & Collections efficiency.
Mar '26
Refund Module / Flow
Eliminates manual refund processing causing TAT breaches and RBI escalations; digitizes refunds for faster turnaround and better CX.
TBD
Cancellations Flow Automation
Standardizes cancellation calculations, policy enforcement and accounting across business lines while removing heavy Fin-Ops dependency.
Mar '26
Pennant/Saarthi — API Based Push Integration
Fixes fragile data pipelines by creating a reliable system-to-system accounting flow and improving data quality and ownership.
Apr '26
Disbursement / Collections / Settlement Accounting (Co-lending)
Establishes automated accounting for co-lending flows to eliminate reconciliation issues and manual posting.
Apr '26
Settlement CG-Pennant Sync Flow
Resolves data mismatches between Collections (CG) and Pennant, enabling real-time loan book accuracy and eliminating manual reconciliation.
Apr '26
Collections Payment Bifurcation (Own Book & Co-lending)
Ensures collections from Razorpay/Setu/NACH are correctly split between Own Book and Co-Lending to avoid accounting inconsistencies.
TBD
24×7 Disbursal Service API
Prevents downtime in disbursement processing and ensures uninterrupted business operations round the clock.
Mar '26
Saarthi Disbursal — Payout Validation
Provides real-time payout status visibility to business users, removing dependency on Finance for manual validation.
TBD
DSA Commission Payout
Eliminates manual payout processing, improving payout timelines, dealer experience and business control.
Apr '26
FT & FinOps (FT)
Lead: Ankit · SAP Automation, Escrow Recon & Accounting Migrations
6 P0 Projects

Aligns accounting with the source-of-truth system to resolve data pipeline inconsistencies and improve reporting reliability.
Mar '26
Holdback Release Automation Integration
Completes the automation loop by integrating holdback accounting with the system, removing manual book-keeping.
Mar '26
Escrow Bank Reconciliation (Collection)
Automates reconciliation of collection escrow accounts to reduce manual effort and improve control.
TBD
Escrow Bank Reconciliation (Disbursal)
Automates reconciliation of disbursement escrow accounts, improving efficiency and reducing operational overhead.
TBD
Invoice Auto Knock-off in SAP
Eliminates manual invoice matching in SAP, reducing effort, errors and delays in ledger updates.
TBD
Document Reversal Feature
Enables system-based reversal of incorrect accounting entries, avoiding manual corrections and improving financial control.

FY26 Delivered — Biz Fin & FP&A

Key achievements across forecasting, dashboards and commercial enablers
✓ AI-driven Pulse & Performance Review Automation
Speed & Efficiency
Led automation of the monthly Pulse & Performance Review dashboard through AI-enabled workflows. Reduced reporting cycle time by 2–3 days, enabled T+0 automated deck generation at exact month-end with improved visual storytelling, and accelerated management review cycles.
✓ Accounting Profit Target Achievement (₹42 Cr PBT)
Growth & Business
Spearheaded cross-functional initiatives across VAS revenue enhancement, technology capitalization optimization, cross-charge rationalization and DA-based decision frameworks to achieve the ₹42 Cr accounting profit target. Supported credit rating improvement initiatives.
✓ Strategic Closure of PFL (Regulatory & Financial Milestone)
₹18 Cr P&L Impact · Regulatory Milestone
Successfully executed the long-pending PFL closure — a strategic regulatory recommendation highlighted by RBI. Delivered ₹18 Cr profitability reflected in books and achieved final commercial settlement of ₹5 Cr. Closed within Q3 FY26 timelines.
✓ Bajaj Co-Lending Partnership & JV Commercial Structuring
Growth & Funding Enabler
Acted as Finance anchor for the Bajaj co-lending program — delivering P&L simulations, financial operations framework and accounting structures for go-live readiness. Currently leading commercial negotiations for Bajaj JV including receivable structuring.
✓ P&L Enhancement with Cost Optimization / VAS Improvement
~₹5.7 Cr Yearly Impact
Drove cost optimization across GST Input, Tech Infrastructure, Bajaj side expenses, VAS rate revision and GST holdback release. Conducted ROI analysis on VAS and revenue streams enabling revenue enhancement opportunities.
✓ Strategic Clearance of Legacy Receivables
₹27+ Cr Receivables Cleared
Reduced Pmaxx legacy receivables from ~₹10 Cr to <₹0.5 Cr, cleared ₹17+ Cr Insurance outstanding within two months, and addressed PL receivables >₹30L aged beyond 90 days. Introduced T+7 invoice issuance cycle from reconciliation date.
✓ Live P&L & Collections Analytics Suite
Analytics & Collections Visibility
Delivered daily live P&L visibility at T+2 lag across revenue and cost lines. Built structured MIS, P&L analytics and strategic insights across LOB businesses, Collections, Suraksha, PMaxx and cross-entity financial performance.
✓ MIS TAT Optimization
T+6 → T+3 · Speed & Efficiency
Reduced MIS turnaround time from T+6 to T+3, significantly improving speed and quality of financial reporting to enable faster leadership decision-making.

FY26 Delivered — Controllership

Audit governance, systems implementation and regulatory compliance
✓ Strengthening Audit & Financial Governance
Governance & Compliance
Statutory Audit closed with nil/minimal remarks; closure improved to Jun FY26 vs Jul FY25
✓ Enhancing Financial Close & Accounting Accuracy (MEC)
Accuracy & Efficiency
Streamlined MEC timelines; PFL head-wise reconciliation; DA accounting closure for Aug '25 tranche
✓ SAP HANA Implementation & Accounting Automation
Systems & Automation
Balance migration; automation of AG flows for Pocket Insurance, ABC, Co-lending & Pennant accounting
✓ Regulatory Compliance & Reporting
Regulatory Readiness
Timely RBI returns; IRDA data sync for last 3 FYs; cross-charge alignment till H1 FY26
✓ Process Optimization — GST 9% Savings Logic
Cost & Efficiency
Accounting change reducing cross-functional effort and improving process efficiency

Collections

✓ Collection Report & EMI Settlement (Bajaj Share)
Reporting

Codified settlement & repo framework to minimize ECL loss

📋 Overview
🛠️ 11 Projects
📊 21 KPIs
🤖 AI Implementation
LEGAL & COMPLIANCE · STRATEGIC INTENT FY27
Compliance shifts from Gatekeeper → Growth Enabler — embedding compliance DNA into every product, partnership, and process from inception.
Delivering ₹60L+ in direct cost savings and avoiding ₹7–8 Cr in infrastructure expenditure through the Sub-KUA model · 4 pillars · 11 projects · 21 KPIs
Direct Cost Saving
₹60L+
Annual · CKYC vendor elimination
~₹5L/month saved
Penalty Risk Avoided
₹250Cr+
DPDP + regulatory exposure
Pre-emptive readiness
Onboarding TAT
48 hrs → <30 min
DIY KYC + V-CIP journey
Login-to-disbursal
Total Investment
₹39–55L
One-time + annual
ROI: 10×+ year 1

Four Strategic Pillars

Pillar 1
Cost Optimization
Vendor dependency → In-house automation → ₹60L annual savings
Pillar 2
Frictionless Digital Growth
Manual KYC → DIY + V-CIP + ULI → 48 hrs to <30 min onboarding
Pillar 3
Regulatory Future-Proofing
Reactive → Anticipatory (DPDP, DLG, AI) → Zero surprises; Day-1 ready
Pillar 4
Governance as a Moat
Ad-hoc → Structured frameworks → Investor & bank-partner ready

Compliance Value Adds — P&L Impact

Value Add Business Impact Measurable Outcome
CKYC In-House Automation₹60L direct annual savingVendor invoice → ₹0
DIY KYC + V-CIP JourneyFaster conversions; high-intent customer captureLogin-to-disbursal TAT <30 min
ULI IntegrationThin-file segment unlocked; fraud reducedNew approvals + fraud rate −30%
DPDP Consent ArchitecturePrevents ₹250Cr+ penalty riskDPDP readiness score >90%
DLG Structuring & MonitoringKeeps fintech partnerships legally safeDLG maintained ≤5% cap
Co-Lending Compliance TemplateEnables 2nd bank partner faster2 active bank partners by FY27
AI in Compliance Ops50%+ reduction in manual effortSOP drafting: days → hours
RBI Advisory FunctionZero regulatory blind spotsAdvisory note within 5 days of circular
Dark Pattern EliminationImproved NPS; reduced complaintsComplaint volume −20%
SBR Threshold MonitoringPrevents overnight governance escalation0 unplanned layer upgrades

11 Compliance Projects — FY27

Across 4 pillars · With deliverables & KPIs
Pillar 1 · Cost Optimization
P1 In-House CKYC & CIC Automation
₹60L/year direct saving
Goal
Eliminate ₹5L/month vendor cost; enable real-time identity verification in-house
Deliverables
◎
API Blueprint: Map LMS fields to CERSAI API; define JSON structure for uploads/responses
Due: Apr 30
◎
Exception Logic Framework: Auto-trigger "Manual Review" for image mismatch / validation errors
Due: Jun 30
◎
Go-Live: In-house CKYC API; vendor contract terminated
Due: Jul 31
◎
Daily Reconciliation SOP: Disbursals vs. CKYC uploads automated matching
Due: Aug 15
◎
Audit Trail Live: API logs with timestamps, response codes & transaction IDs
Due: Aug 31
KPIs
Metric Baseline FY27 Target
Vendor Cost (CKYC/CIC) ₹5L/month ₹0
CKYC Upload Success Rate Not tracked >98%
Exception Resolution TAT Manual / untracked <4 hours
Recon Discrepancy Rate Not tracked <0.1%
Pillar 2 · Frictionless Digital Growth
P2 DIY KYC Journey — CKYC + V-CIP Triangulation
Revenue from faster conversions
Goal
Compress onboarding from 48 hrs to <30 mins; enable paperless remote KYC for Tier 3/4 markets
Deliverables
◎
Triangulation Model Design: 3-layer CKYC + V-CIP + OCR/AI identity framework
Due: Apr 30
◎
RBI KYC Master Directions (2025) audit: GPS capture, audio/video trails, 10-year retention
Due: May 31
◎
Exception / Assisted DIY SOP: Auto-route liveness failures to agent-assisted mode
Due: Jun 30
◎
Digital Documentation Framework: Paperless capture, validation & storage
Due: Jul 31
◎
Go-Live: Full DIY Journey for Personal Loan
Due: Aug 31
◎
Remote KYC enabled for Tier 3/4 markets without physical presence
Due: Oct 31
KPIs
Metric Baseline FY27 Target
Onboarding TAT 40–60 mins <30 mins
DIY Journey Completion Rate Not measured >80%
V-CIP Liveness Success Rate Not measured >90%
Physical Document Dependency High 0%
P3 ULI (Unified Lending Interface) Integration
New segment access + fraud −30%
Goal
Enable real-time credit underwriting via verified data sources; unlock thin-file customer segment
Deliverables
◎
IT / Cyber Security Audit Certificate obtained (hard ULI pre-requisite)
Due: May 31
◎
ULI Lender Registration on RBI Innovation Hub portal
Due: Jun 30
◎
DPDP-aligned Granular Consent Architecture: purpose + source + duration
Due: Jul 31
◎
ULI Data Governance Framework: access controls, 7-day data discard policy
