AI-Driven Implementation Playbook

CARS24 FS · FY 2026–27 AOP · Zero tech-team dependency · Claude + Sheets + Zapier
📋
Ready Prompts
Copy-paste prompts for every task
⚡
Auto Flows
Step-by-step Zapier & no-code workflows
🛠
Zero-code Tools
Only Claude, Sheets & Zapier needed
P1 In-House CKYC & CIC Automation 🤖 Partial AI
AI Note
Core API integration needs tech once. Everything else is Claude-driven.
What Claude Can Do
Generate complete API Blueprint
Upload CERSAI docs → Claude produces the full JSON field mapping spec, error codes, retry logic and upload flow document
Draft Exception Logic Framework
Claude creates the full decision tree: image mismatch → auto-review, validation error → manual queue, success → proceed
Daily Reconciliation Checker
Paste today's disbursal list + CKYC upload log → Claude flags every mismatch with category and recommended action
Audit Trail SOP
Claude generates the complete API logging specification and compliance monitoring checklist for regulatory inspection readiness
Weekly Exception Trend Report
Paste 7-day exception log → Claude produces a structured trend report identifying top failure patterns and root causes
Implementation Flow
1
Set up Claude Project: 'CKYC Operations'
Upload CERSAI API documentation, RBI KYC Master Directions, and your internal LMS field list. Claude now has persistent context for all CKYC tasks.
Claude Projects
2
Generate API Blueprint (Week 1)
Prompt Claude to map your LMS fields to CERSAI standards and produce a spec document. Share this with tech — they build to your spec.
Claude.ai
3
Create Exception Decision Tree
Ask Claude to produce a flowchart-style SOP for every CERSAI API response code. Operations team follows this.
Claude.ai
4
Automate Daily Reconciliation
Each morning, export disbursal list + CKYC upload log (CSV). Paste both into Claude with the recon prompt. Claude flags exceptions in seconds.
Claude + Sheets
5
Weekly Monitoring Report
Every Friday, paste week's exception log. Claude generates a structured report for management with pattern analysis.
Claude.ai
6
Zapier: Auto-alert on recon gaps
Zapier monitors your Google Sheet recon tracker → if a mismatch column is flagged → auto-sends email alert to compliance team.
Zapier
P2 DIY KYC Journey — V-CIP + CKYC Triangulation 🤖 Full AI
AI Note
All SOPs, compliance checklists, agent training and audit frameworks can be Claude-generated without any tech dependency.
What Claude Can Do
RBI KYC Master Directions Audit
Paste the MD text → Claude generates a point-by-point compliance checklist mapped to your DIY journey with gap analysis
V-CIP SOP: Liveness Failure Handling
Claude creates the complete agent SOP for when liveness check fails — decision tree, script, escalation matrix
Assisted DIY Mode Script
Claude generates the full customer interaction script for your sales agents guiding customers through assisted DIY mode
10-Year Retention Policy
Claude drafts the audio/video audit trail retention policy, storage classification, and destruction schedule per RBI requirements
Agent Certification Training Deck
Claude creates a complete training module for agents on V-CIP regulations, dos and don'ts, and escalation procedures
Implementation Flow
1
Upload RBI KYC Master Directions to Claude Project
Create a Claude Project: 'KYC Compliance'. Upload the full Master Directions PDF. Claude now acts as your KYC compliance expert.
Claude Projects
2
Generate Compliance Gap Checklist
Ask Claude to compare your current onboarding flow against MD requirements. Produces a 50-point checklist with severity ratings.
Claude.ai
3
Generate all SOPs in one session
Use the SOP generator prompt to produce: liveness failure SOP, assisted DIY SOP, exception escalation SOP, documentation SOP — all in one session.
Claude.ai
4
Create Agent Training Materials
Claude generates a complete training pack: regulation summary, dos/don'ts, customer scripts, FAQ for agents, quick reference card.
Claude.ai
5
Build Audit Trail Template
Claude creates the standardized audit trail log template — every V-CIP session logged with: timestamp, GPS, video file reference, liveness score, outcome.
Claude + Sheets
P7 DPDP Implementation 🤖 Full AI
AI Note
All DPDP documentation, consent architecture design, vendor DPA templates, and readiness assessments can be fully generated by compliance using Claude — zero external consultant needed.
What Claude Can Do
Plain-Language Privacy Notice
Claude rewrites your current T&C privacy section into clear, DPDP-compliant language with purpose-specific consent requests
3-Level Consent Architecture Design
Claude produces the full consent architecture spec: L1 Loan / L2 Marketing / L3 Partner sharing with UI copy and audit trail requirements
Vendor DPA Template
Claude generates a DPDP-compliant Data Processing Agreement template covering purpose limitation, retention, deletion and breach notification
Right to Erasure SOP
Complete SOP covering how to handle deletion requests from all systems, backups, vendor environments within 30-day statutory timeline
Board Readiness Presentation
Claude generates a structured Board paper: DPDP overview, our current gaps, implementation plan, and proposed sign-offs required
Implementation Flow
1
Create Claude Project: 'DPDP Compliance'
Upload the DPDP Act 2023, the anticipated Rules draft, and your current privacy notice. Claude becomes your DPDP expert advisor.
Claude Projects
2
Generate Consent Architecture
Ask Claude to map all data collection points in your app/website and produce the full consent architecture with tiered consent levels.
Claude.ai
3
Draft all Vendor DPAs
For each critical vendor, paste their service scope. Claude generates a customized DPA in 5 minutes instead of 3 days with a lawyer.
Claude.ai
4
Build Obligation Tracker in Sheets
Ask Claude to generate a structured Google Sheet with all DPDP obligations, owners, deadlines and status tracking formula.
Claude + Sheets
5
Monthly DPDP Status Report
Each month, update the tracker. Claude generates a Board-ready DPDP status report from the raw tracker data.
Claude.ai
P10 AI in Compliance Operations ⭐ 🤖 Core AI
AI Note
This is the meta-project — it's about building the AI muscle for everything else. Start here if you haven't already.
What Claude Can Do
RBI Circular Daily Digest
Claude reads new RBI circular → produces: plain-language summary, business impact for CARS24 FS, compliance obligations triggered, recommended actions
Contract Clause Red-Flag Review
Paste any MCLA, LSP agreement, or vendor contract → Claude flags every clause that violates or is missing RBI-mandated requirements
Board Note Auto-Draft
Paste the raw inputs (circular text, action taken, current status) → Claude generates a polished Board/management note in 2 minutes
Complaint Pattern Detection
Export CRM complaint log → Claude categorizes by type, identifies repeat patterns, flags potential regulatory risk clusters
SOP First Draft Generator
Describe the process → Claude generates a complete SOP with purpose, scope, procedure, responsibilities, and records to maintain
Implementation Flow
1
Build your Compliance Knowledge Base
Create one Claude Project per domain: KYC, Digital Lending, Data Privacy, Recovery. Upload the relevant RBI circulars and MDs to each. This is your always-on compliance brain.
Claude Projects
2
Set up Daily Regulatory Monitor
Each morning: open Claude → use the Daily Digest prompt → paste any new RBI/DPDP updates. Takes 5 minutes. Outputs action plan for the day.
Claude.ai
3
Automate weekly reporting
Every Friday: export key data (recon logs, complaint counts, KPI numbers) → paste into Claude with the weekly report prompt → send to management. Replace 3 hours of work with 15 minutes.
Claude + Sheets
4
Contract review workflow
When a new contract comes in: paste into Claude with the contract review prompt → get a flagged version in minutes instead of days.
Claude.ai
P11 RBI Advisory Function ⭐ 🤖 Full AI
AI Note
The advisory note drafting, obligation tracker maintenance, and knowledge base build — all fully Claude-powered. This is the highest-ROI AI use case in compliance.
What Claude Can Do
Advisory Note Generator
Paste a new RBI circular → Claude produces a complete advisory note: plain-language summary, business impact for CARS24 FS, obligations triggered, action plan, timeline
Regulatory Obligation Tracker
Claude generates and maintains a Google Sheet tracker: circular, obligations, owner, action required, deadline, status — updated each time a new advisory note is issued
Solution Design Memo
When a new regulation creates a business constraint, describe the constraint → Claude proposes 3 compliant solution options with pros/cons for leadership decision
Knowledge Base Article Writer
For each circular, Claude generates a structured KB article: background, key changes, CARS24 FS impact, what we did, outcome
Board Quarterly Regulatory Update
Claude compiles all advisory notes from the quarter into a structured Board paper covering: regulatory landscape, compliance status, upcoming risks, team actions
Implementation Flow
1
Set up Regulatory Monitoring
Google Alert for "RBI circular" + "RBI notification" → email to you daily. Takes 2 minutes to set up. Free.
Google Alerts
2
Issue Advisory Note within 5 days
When a new circular drops: paste into Claude → use the Advisory Note Generator prompt → first draft in 10 minutes → review → send to leadership.
Claude.ai
3
Update Obligation Tracker
After issuing the advisory note: paste the obligations section → Claude updates the tracker format → copy to your Google Sheet master tracker.
Claude + Sheets
4
Archive in Knowledge Base
Each advisory note → Claude generates the KB article version → add to your running knowledge base Google Doc. Searchable by anyone in the team.
Claude.ai + Sheets
📋 Overview & Strategy
🚫 D2C / B2C
🤝 B2B2C
🏢 B2B
🌐 Global Players
BUYBACK · RVI · CHANNEL STRATEGY
Buyback 101 — Strategy Dashboard
D2C/B2C · B2B2C · B2B channels | 20 global players | March 2026 | Internal strategy reference
3
GTM Segments
16+
Channel Use Cases
20
Global Players
2 Live
5+ Explore Priority
🔑 Core Product Insight
Buyback should never be pitched as a standalone VAS to an external partner. It creates value only when: (1) bundled with Loan Insurance in internal NBFC channels where mandate is top-down, (2) embedded as a structural solution for a B2B partner's balance sheet risk (balloon EOT, EV depreciation, fleet liquidation), or (3) used as a new-car sales accelerant by an OEM launching in a competitive EV market. The product needs a mandate or a structural hook — never a cold Cx pitch.

Strategic Decision Matrix — Channel Priority View

🚫 Discontinue
D2C App — standalone, low perceived value
PDI Channel — trust deficit, repeated drop-off
BFL / Piramal UCF standalone — didn't pick up
Dealer network — negative response, price ceiling
Standalone VAS pitch to any external partner
✅ Continue / Scale
All internal NBFC — bundled with LI
BFL NCF — 10% penetration, maintain
BFL Flex / Balloon — going live, watch closely
GS Cash bundle — 30% attach achieved briefly
RGIC / Go-DIGIT — liquidation partner, EV focus
🔍 Explore Next
Nissan EV — new launch confidence product
OEM EV assured buyback — match MG/Maruti/Skoda
AssureKit bundle — car care program angle
Leasing cos — guaranteed liquidation at price
KOGTA / balloon lenders — EOT risk hedge
📋 Executive Summary
Buyback / RVI products face a structural challenge: low perceived value due to uncertain, future-dated risk. The product works best when bundled with a primary high-penetration product (e.g. Loan Insurance), offered via a B2B2C channel, or when the counterparty has a clear structural need (balloon loan EOT exposure, EV depreciation, fleet liquidation). Standalone D2C and dealer-led models have repeatedly failed. Priority vectors: B2B2C (internal NBFC + BFL Flex) and B2B (OEM EV, leasing, insurance RV partnerships).

D2C / B2C — Standalone Product

Push product · Customer is the decision maker · Cx decides whether to buy buyback independently
💡 Why D2C Fails
The fundamental problem is that the risk being hedged (future resale depreciation) feels abstract and non-urgent at point of purchase. Unlike warranty or motor insurance, the customer doesn't feel protected immediately. PDI compounds this — trust in Cars24's quality must exist before Cx will pay to sell a car back to them later. The product needs either a structural mandate or a completely different framing (upgrade enabler, not risk hedge).
2 channels tested · Both discontinued
ChannelPain Point / Use CaseExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
D2C App 1/ Peace of mind re: resale anxiety
2/ Frequent upgrade — structured method to enable Cx to trade in every 2–3 years		 No external comparable found D2C live on app for selling buyback alongside assisted calling — Low to no perceived value. Uncertain & insignificant future risk — Cx doesn't hedge it right now. — Discontinue
PDI Channel Resale confidence at PDI — when Cx is in buying mindset and about to take delivery No — tested 100+ customers, 5–10 different pitches; price slashed to ₹2k (₹10k deducted at claim) Multiple pitch variations at PDI. Instant-gratification pricing tested. — 1/ PDI issues (delays, poor quality) → drop-off
2/ Trust deficit with Cars24 post bad PDI experience
3/ Low perceived value — no intent to sell yet, risk feels distant		 — Discontinue

B2B2C — Bundled / Mandatory Push Product

Seller is the decision maker · They mandate or bundle the product · Cx is end beneficiary but not the buyer
5 channel groups · 11 use cases
Cars24 — Platform Retention
ChannelPain Point / Use CaseExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
Cars24 GS Cash Retention / customer trust — keep Cx coming back for next purchase Spinny provides ABC (Assured Buyback Cert) free with GS sales Standalone in GS Cash; bundled in Care+ 30% attach in GS Cash achieved briefly with top-down push Deprioritised — other higher-margin VAS in the kitty Depends on VAS margin stack and whether it remains a strategic priority vs other VAS Push only if central priority
Internal — Loans24 / NBFC Channels
ChannelPain Point / Use CaseExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
All internal NBFC (Refi + LI bundle) Margin / revenue — buyback earns higher margin vs most other VAS internally No like-for-like comparable Standalone pocket product in Refi; bundled with LI (100% penetration product) in all other channels Works with mandate or when bundled with LI. ₹2.5k standalone failed → ₹1k bundled with LI succeeded. Standalone is a difficult sell every time. Cx doesn't perceive future value without a hook. Channel attaches because of higher internal margin and top-down push. Both are required. Continue internally
OEM Partners
ChannelPain Point / Use CaseExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
OEM Faster switch / upgrade — structured method to enable Cx to trade in every 2–3 years Merc, BMW, Audi, Lexus, Toyota pursue this globally Tried with Honda — dropped off. KIA on verge of signing but dropped due to EV ineligibility. — Low partner prioritisation. OEMs won't do it for marginal revenue — only for car sales volume. This will eventually be a Cx product — needs Cx conviction first Long-term watch
OEM — New ICE / EV Launch New launch Cx confidence — reduce depreciation anxiety at launch to drive sales MG, Maruti Suzuki, Skoda Superb, VinFast — all have examples in India Not yet tried in this framing — — Strong external precedent. Most effective at launch window. Timing is critical. Explore
OEM — Affordability / Balloon EMI Lower EMI / balloon — defer value to end of term making monthly payments optically smaller (PCP / GFV model) Luxury OEMs do this in-house (Merc Star Agility, BMW 360°) Not tried — only few Indian OEMs have captive finance arms — — India market nascent for balloon/PCP structures. Captive finance arm is a prerequisite. Long-term watch
External Lenders
ChannelPain Point / Use CaseExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
Lenders Margin / distribution income — VAS fee to lender at each buyback disbursement No like-for-like product in India BFL NCF: active. UCF (BFL + Piramal): tried. BFL NCF: 10% penetration BFL UCF and Piramal UCF didn't pick up Depends on ASP, margin, ease of saleability vs other VAS in their kitty Continue BFL NCF
BFL Flex (Balloon) Disbursals growth — BFL uses buyback to make balloon product viable and grow volume — Going live — buyback embedded in BFL Flex product — — Monitor closely at go-live. If balloon structure flies, partner with other balloon lenders. Going Live
Insurance Partners
ChannelPain Point / Use CaseExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
Insurance — Topline Growth Topline growth — insurer bundles buyback as add-on to grow premium income — Bundling with other primary products — — Exploring bundling with AssureKit as a car care program Explore
Insurance — Differentiation Add-on product differentiation — distinguish motor policy vs competitors — Not yet tried — — Explore pocket products. Low ASP, high margin. Charge Cx only at claim time — reduces upfront resistance. Explore
Insurance — Renewal Surge Renewal attach — bundle buyback at renewal to increase renewal rate and cross-sell opportunity — Not yet tried — — Yet to discuss with any partner. Renewal timing: Cx is already re-evaluating their car situation. Explore
Dealer Network
ChannelPain Point / Use CaseExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
Dealer — Increase Cars Sold Dealer offers buyback guarantee to reduce Cx hesitation and close deals faster — Spoke to 10+ dealers. Negative to neutral response. — Dealers not interested in small margins. Don't want same car back. Risk of dealer inflating sale price. Fundamental conflict: dealer wants to move inventory forward, not receive it back Discontinue
Dealer — Margin per Car Dealer earns fee for selling buyback as bundled add-on at point of car sale — Spoke to 10+ dealers. Negative response. — Customer won't pay >40–50 bps. Price ceiling too low to be attractive to dealer. — Discontinue

B2B — Business Pays, Business Decides

Business has structural balance sheet / risk exposure · They pay · May or may not pass cost to end customer
6 channel groups · 12 use cases
OEM — Structural Balance Sheet Plays
Channel / Use CasePain PointExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
OEM — Captive Finance (Balloon/PCP) Balloon risk / uncapped EOT exposure — captive carries residual risk on PCP portfolios. RVI backstops this. Lower cost of capital if exposure is insured. RVI Group — standard globally for OEM captives Not tried directly. Tried Hyundai ↔ HDFC ↔ Cars24 tripartite structure. — Multiple partners + no market precedent dissolved the deal. India: only few OEMs have captive finance. Simpler 2-party structure needed. Precedent is the blocker. Hold (wait and watch)
OEM — EV / ICE New Launch New launch Cx confidence — OEM uses buyback to eliminate depreciation anxiety and stimulate purchases Valeur (Cherry Group, Australia) — finance-agnostic GVB model Not tried — actively exploring — — — Explore — Nissan
OEM — EV Product Differentiation Match competitor — OEM needs assured buyback to stay competitive (MG, Maruti, Skoda Superb, VinFast all have it) All Indian EV OEMs with assured buyback Nil so far — — Competitive pressure from MG/Lockton/Zuno model is the forcing function Explore EVs broadly
Lenders — EOT & Balloon Risk
Channel / Use CasePain PointExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
Lender — EOT / Balloon (Piramal, Bajaj, KOGTA) EOT risk — balloon + repo exposure, uncapped residual at end of term. Assured buyback backstops if car value falls below balloon. RVI Group, Valeur (Australia) Repo guarantees tried with Piramal and Bajaj. Pitched to KOGTA. Piramal resonated Bajaj didn't resonate. Lender use case limited if lender pays independently. Only balloon loans create high EOT exposure requiring assured buyback backstop Explore — BFL Flex as proof point
Lender — Used Car Valuation with Guarantee (Bajaj) Insured valuation floor on used car collateral — reduces underwriting risk for lender Valeur / AUTOINSPECKT8 Very initial conversation with Bajaj — Indian Blue Book widely accepted and trusted. Lenders are accustomed to this risk. No standalone play. Can be bundled as add-on but not a primary value prop. No standalone play
Leasing Companies
Channel / Use CasePain PointExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
Leasing — New Car Operational RV risk + reduce balance sheet exposure — leasing co carries residual risk, needs guaranteed liquidation prices RVI Group, Tokio Marine, Matrix, Valeur Not explored — — Leasing in India = tax saving (higher payments), NOT lower monthly cost. Structurally different from Western markets. Be liquidation partner at guaranteed prices
Leasing — Car Subscription RV floor for subscription pricing — short-cycle subscription cos cycle cars every 12–36 months, need RV floor RVI Group, Tokio Marine, Matrix, Valeur Not explored — — Can work for short-cycle subscription partners seeking lower subscription fees (enabled by RV guarantee) Explore short-cycle subscription cos
Insurance — Structural / RV Risk
Channel / Use CasePain PointExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
RGIC / Go-DIGIT Risk reduction for claims — insurer uses C24 for valuation insights + guaranteed liquidation for total loss / high-claim scenarios Valuation insights by RVI Group, Matrix Partnership with Go-DIGIT — for valuation insights AND liquidation partner role Liquidation partner role resonated more than valuation insights EVs is where insurer primarily needs support — Explore RV partnerships — new ICE + EV OEMs
Insurance — Battery / EV Specialized Hedge Specialized risk hedge — EV battery RV insurance / exposure that standard actuarial tables don't cover Munich Re, Swiss Re — only insure battery for EVs (performance warranty, not RV) Nil — — — Monitor EV market
Dealer — Inventory Risk
Channel / Use CasePain PointExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
Dealer — Dead Stock Risk Guaranteed exit on aging inventory — dealer gets a price floor to retire dead stock Nil Nil Nil Operationally heavy. Very high fraud risk — dealer can game valuations. — Discontinue
Fleet Owners
Channel / Use CasePain PointExternal Product?What We TriedWhat WorkedWhat Didn't WorkRemarks / NotesWay Forward
Fleet — Liquidation of Old Vehicles Guaranteed price floor for fleet operator to retire aging vehicles at a known value Nil Nil — — High fraud risk. Fleet operators can manipulate vehicle condition. High risk, low priority
Fleet — ABS / Lower Cost of Finance Fleet uses insured RV as collateral to raise cheaper debt (ABS / asset-backed structures) ABS in the West (RVI Group, Matrix, etc.) Nil — — Indian market not mature enough for ABS on used car fleets yet Monitor market maturity

Global Player Intelligence — v5

20 players across 6 sections · Scale (1–5 ●) = RVI/buyback business size only
Demand Side (4) RVI Insurers (4) Broker / Structuring (3) Adjacent (2) Captive / GFV (5) OEM Buyback India (2)
⚠️ Demand-side players (Ayvens, Arval, Ford Credit, GM/Toyota) are RVI buyers, not sellers. Their scale determines the market's size.
D · Demand Side — Leasing Giants These are not RVI providers — they are RVI buyers
D1 Ayvens (ALD + LeasePlan) Demand
#1 global fleet leasing · 3.2M vehicles managed · Société Générale · Paris · France · Europe · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Established ALD Automotive 1968. LeasePlan 1963. Merged May 2023. Rebranded Ayvens Oct 2023.
Full-service operational vehicle leasing — fixed monthly payments covering maintenance, repairs, insurance. Residual value = "largest risk" managed via internal RV modeling and multi-channel remarketing. 600,000 vehicles sold per year via remarketing platform; 24,000 active traders in 36 countries. World's largest multi-brand EV fleet: 636,000 units (June 2025).
Pivots
May 2023: ALD completed €4.8B acquisition of LeasePlan creating world's #1 fleet leasing company at 3.3M vehicles. 2023–24: CEO acknowledged adjusting pricing through reduction in residual values for EVs. Also negotiated OEM pricing protection clauses.
Partners & Pain
Corporate fleet clients (400,000+) outsource residual value risk entirely to Ayvens. OEMs: Ayvens uses ~500k+ vehicles/yr purchasing scale to negotiate pricing protection against EV depreciation. ⚠️ Ayvens is a buyer, not a seller, of RVI.
Scale
3.2M vehicles managed (June 2025). #1 in 29 of 42 countries. Pre-tax profit €994M (2024).
D2 Arval (BNP Paribas) Demand
#2 global fleet leasing · 1.9M vehicles financed · BNP Paribas division · Paris · France · Europe · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Founded 1989 as Arval Service Lease. Fully owned by BNP Paribas.
Full-service vehicle leasing — average contract 45 months. Internal RV modeling: statistical scores per vehicle, reviewed monthly. Arval Re-lease: used-car leasing (+30% growth in 2024) as RV risk mitigation. 800,000 vehicles with Arval Connect telemetry for proactive maintenance and value protection.
Pivots
2024: BNP Paribas confirmed Arval organic revenues grew 17.9% in 2023; total revenues declined due to normalisation of used-car prices. EV residual values identified as primary risk challenge. 2025: fleet reached 1,894,865 vehicles (+5.5%). Net income €645M.
Partners & Pain
BNP Paribas provides ~70% of Arval's funding (~€30B). 400,000+ corporate clients outsource fleet and RV risk entirely. ⚠️ Arval is a buyer of RVI capacity, not a seller.
Scale
1,894,865 vehicles financed (end 2025). Total revenue €3,627M (2024). Ratings A/F1 (Fitch) · A–/A2 (S&P).
D3 Ford Credit (Ford Motor Credit Company) Demand
OEM captive finance · Pioneer of RV modeling in US auto leasing · USA · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Ford Motor Credit Company founded 1959.
Auto leasing with residual value set at contract start — customer protected from RV downside. Balloon finance products. Uses internal residual value models; selectively purchases RVI for portfolio hedges. ABS issuances — securitises lease portfolios, distributing RV risk to capital markets.
Pivots
No RVI/buyback-specific pivots found in public sources.
Partners & Pain
Retail auto lease customers: protected from RV downside in closed-end lease — if car is worth less than set residual at return, Ford Credit absorbs the loss. ABS investors: routinely securitises lease portfolios. ⚠️ Specific RVI insurer relationships not publicly disclosed.
Scale
Total managed receivables ~$156B (2024). RVI-specific usage not publicly disclosed.
D4 GM Financial / Toyota Financial Services Demand
US OEM captive finance — residual risk pioneers · USA · Japan · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
GM Financial (via AmeriCredit acquisition 2010); Toyota Financial Services established 1982.
GM Financial: full-service captive — leasing, balloon, and retail finance for GM brands globally. Toyota Financial Services: leasing and finance for Toyota and Lexus. Both use internal RV models developed over decades of US leasing experience. Both access capital markets via ABS.
Pivots
No RVI/buyback-specific pivots found in public sources.
Partners & Pain
GM/Toyota dealership networks: captives enable competitive lease offers. ABS investors: both captives routinely securitise lease receivables. ⚠️ Specific RVI insurer relationships not publicly disclosed.
Scale
GM Financial total assets ~$115B (2024). Toyota Financial Services total managed assets ~$200B+.
I · RVI Insurers — Pure Play These companies sell residual value insurance as their primary product
I1 RVI Group RVI Insurer
Pioneer pure-play RVI insurer · $59B+ insured since 1989 · London · UK · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Established 1989. The original standalone RVI insurer. Has insured over $59 billion of residual value risk globally.
Residual value insurance for OEM captives, leasing companies, and fleet operators. Covers PCP/GFV balloon risk, fleet RV exposure, and car subscription RV floors. Works across 20+ countries.
Pivots
Recently expanded into EV RV insurance as OEMs and leasing cos face higher EV depreciation uncertainty. Structural demand driver: OEM electrification is creating the largest RVI opportunity since 1989.
Partners & Pain
OEM captive finance arms (global); Major leasing companies (ALD/Ayvens, Arval, Element Fleet); Fleet operators seeking guaranteed liquidation prices. Primary pain: EV residual value uncertainty.
Scale
$59B+ total insured since 1989. Exact annual premium revenue not publicly disclosed.
I2 Valeur Financial / Valeur Group RVI Insurer
Finance-agnostic GVB model · Cherry Group connection · Australia · Sydney · Australia · Asia-Pacific
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Valeur Financial is the RVI/GVB (Guaranteed Vehicle Buyback) arm within the Cherry Group ecosystem (Chery Automotive). Operates in Australia with a finance-agnostic model.
Guaranteed Vehicle Buyback (GVB) — not tied to captive finance. Works with any lender and any dealer network. Core product: OEM uses Valeur to offer guaranteed future buyback price at time of new car sale, reducing depreciation anxiety for buyer.
Pivots
Finance-agnostic model is the key differentiator vs OEM captive GFV programs — Valeur works even when the OEM doesn't have a captive finance arm. Has been deployed in Australian market for new EV/ICE launches.
Partners & Pain
OEMs without captive finance seeking competitive launch differentiation. Dealers seeking to close deals faster. Lenders seeking structured balloon products with RV backstop.
Scale
Scale limited to Australia/APAC. Revenue and insured volume not publicly disclosed.
I3 Matrix Risk Consultants RVI Insurer
Specialist RVI structuring and insurance · UK-based · London · UK · Europe
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
UK-based specialist in RVI structuring, pricing, and insurance placement. Works at the intersection of automotive finance and insurance markets.
RVI product structuring and pricing for leasing companies, OEM captives, and fleet operators. Acts as both insurer and structuring advisor. Also provides valuation intelligence services for total loss and high-claim scenarios.
Pivots
Growing role in EV fleet RV structuring as European leasing cos demand more sophisticated RV models for EVs. Also expanding into car subscription RV floor products.
Partners & Pain
European leasing companies (including Ayvens, Arval peers). OEM captives seeking RVI placement. Insurance companies seeking valuation intelligence for total loss claims.
Scale
Exact scale not publicly disclosed. Operates primarily in European markets.
I4 Tokio Marine (Auto RVI division) RVI Insurer
Global P&C insurer with dedicated auto RVI capability · Tokyo · Japan · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Tokio Marine is a global P&C insurer (Top 10 globally by premium). Has a dedicated auto/fleet RVI division operating in multiple markets.
Residual value insurance for leasing companies and fleet operators. Works alongside primary motor/fleet insurance products. Core capability in Japan (domestic market) and expanding in Southeast Asia and Europe.
Pivots
Expanding auto RVI capability as Japanese OEMs and leasing companies bring EV fleets to scale. Partnership with leasing cos for fleet-level RV programs.
Partners & Pain
Japanese leasing companies and fleet operators. OEMs with captive finance in Japan. ⚠️ Specific product terms and pricing not publicly disclosed for auto RVI division.
Scale
Tokio Marine total net premiums ~¥2.5T (2024). Auto RVI specific volume not disclosed.
B · Broker / Structuring Layer These players structure RVI deals — they may not hold risk themselves
B1 AUTOINSPECKT8 Broker
Digital vehicle inspection + valuation intelligence for lenders · UK · Europe
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
UK-based digital vehicle inspection and valuation platform. Provides standardised vehicle condition data for lenders, insurers, and leasing companies.
Digital inspection reports for vehicle valuations at point of origination and end-of-term. Structured data for underwriting RVI and balloon products. Partners with lenders for pre-owned vehicle collateral assessment.
Pivots
Expanding from UK into European markets. Targeting integration with RVI insurers to provide real-time vehicle condition data for more accurate RV pricing.
Partners & Pain
UK lenders using RVI (for balloon/PCP). Leasing companies assessing end-of-term vehicle condition. ⚠️ Very initial conversation with Bajaj in India — Indian Blue Book is widely accepted.
Scale
UK-focused. Revenue and market share not publicly disclosed.
B2 Lockton (Auto RVI brokerage) Broker
Specialist insurance broker with dedicated auto RVI practice · Kansas City · USA · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Lockton is the world's largest privately held insurance broker. Has a dedicated auto and mobility practice including RVI brokerage.
RVI placement for OEMs, leasing companies, and fleet operators. Brokers risk to capacity providers (RVI Group, Matrix, Tokio Marine). Notable: Lockton brokers the MG Motor India assured buyback program in partnership with Zuno (Edelweiss) — the most cited India RVI reference.
Pivots
Active in India via MG Motor assured buyback program. MG Motor India uses Lockton to broker the buyback risk to Zuno (formerly Edelweiss General Insurance). This is the primary India RVI reference case.
Partners & Pain
OEMs seeking turnkey RVI solutions (source capacity + structure the product). Lockton-MG-Zuno tripartite is the India template. ⚠️ Lockton does not hold risk — it places it with insurers.
Scale
Lockton global revenue ~$3.5B (2023). Auto RVI specific volume not disclosed.
B3 Zuno (Edelweiss General Insurance) Broker
RVI capacity provider for India OEM programs · MG Motor case · Mumbai · India
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Zuno (formerly Edelweiss General Insurance) is the Indian insurance company providing RVI capacity for OEM assured buyback programs in India.
Provides residual value insurance capacity for OEM-branded assured buyback products in India. Currently: MG Motor India assured buyback program (structured by Lockton, capacity from Zuno). Acts as the risk-bearing insurer in the Lockton-OEM-Zuno tripartite structure.
Pivots
Partnership with MG Motor India (via Lockton) is the current live India RVI reference. Model: OEM markets the assured buyback, Lockton structures the risk, Zuno holds it. This is the only known live OEM RVI program in India.
Partners & Pain
MG Motor India (primary OEM client). Other OEMs with India EV launches (Maruti Suzuki Futuro, Skoda Superb, VinFast) may follow similar structures. ⚠️ Exact terms and RVI premium pricing not publicly disclosed.
Scale
India-focused. Only known live OEM RVI insurer in India. Total premium volume not disclosed.
A · Adjacent — Reinsurance Giants Adjacent to RVI — these players insure the insurers and have limited direct auto RVI exposure
A1 Munich Re (Great Lakes Insurance SE) Adjacent
Global reinsurer with niche EV battery RVI capability · Munich · Germany · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Munich Re is the world's largest reinsurer. Great Lakes Insurance SE is its subsidiary. Limited direct auto RVI — primarily operates in battery/EV performance warranty, not residual value insurance.
Battery performance warranty insurance for EV OEMs — guarantees battery performance (capacity retention) rather than residual value. This is subtly different from RVI: performance warranty = battery works as specified, RVI = car retains its market value.
Pivots
Expanding EV battery warranty programs globally as EV adoption accelerates. OEMs use Munich Re/Great Lakes to backstop battery performance warranties offered to customers.
Partners & Pain
EV OEMs (BMW, Mercedes, VW Group, etc.) seeking battery performance warranty backstops. ⚠️ Munich Re specifically does NOT insure EV residual value — only battery performance. This is a common misunderstanding.
Scale
Munich Re total premiums ~€58B (2024). EV battery warranty specific volume not disclosed.
A2 Swiss Re (Auto / Mobility) Adjacent
Global reinsurer with auto-adjacent RVI capability · Zurich · Switzerland · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Swiss Re is the second-largest reinsurer globally. Has an auto and mobility practice but — similar to Munich Re — primarily operates in battery performance warranty rather than RV insurance.
Battery performance warranty and EV ecosystem risk products. Residual value insurance for auto at smaller scale than specialist RVI players. Connected mobility and cyber risk for automotive OEMs.
Pivots
Expanding EV risk capabilities as electrification creates new insurance categories. Swiss Re Institute actively publishes research on EV residual value risk as a growing insurance market.
Partners & Pain
Global auto OEMs and their captive finance arms. Leasing companies seeking reinsurance for RVI portfolios. ⚠️ Swiss Re's auto RVI is less prominent than its battery warranty positioning.
Scale
Swiss Re total premiums ~$47B (2024). Auto RVI specific volume not publicly disclosed.
C · Captive / GFV Programs OEM-captive guaranteed future value programs — structural demand drivers
C1 Mercedes-Benz Financial Services (Star Agility / AgilityCare) Captive / GFV
Luxury OEM captive PCP/GFV pioneer · Germany · Stuttgart · Germany · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Mercedes-Benz Financial Services has offered Guaranteed Future Value (GFV) products since the early 2000s under Star Agility (UK) and similar names globally.
Star Agility (UK): GFV-based PCP — customer pays lower monthly EMIs, guaranteed future value at end of term. Customer has 3 options at end: return car (GFV holds), buy outright, or use as deposit on new Mercedes. AgilityCare bundles service, insurance, and GFV into one monthly payment.
Pivots
Expanding PCP/GFV to electric vehicles (EQ range) with separate EV-specific RV modeling. Wider adoption in Asian markets including India via captive MBFS.
Partners & Pain
Mercedes-Benz customers (luxury segment): GFV makes luxury cars more accessible via lower monthly payments. ⚠️ Mercedes-Benz MBFS is the dominant captive GFV model in luxury — reference for what a successful program looks like.
Scale
Mercedes-Benz global unit sales ~2M (2024). MBFS GFV penetration not publicly disclosed by market.
C2 BMW Group Financial Services (BMW 360° Excellence) Captive / GFV
Luxury OEM captive · BMW Group · Munich · Germany · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
BMW Group Financial Services offers GFV-based finance products including BMW 360° Excellence — bundling finance, insurance, service, and guaranteed future value into one monthly payment.
BMW 360° Excellence: all-in-one monthly payment covering BMW finance (GFV-based), comprehensive insurance, and service plan. Designed to reduce customer hesitation on total cost of ownership. EV-specific GFV products for iX, i4, i7 range.
Pivots
Accelerating EV GFV programs as BMW Group transitions to 50% electric by 2030. EV RV management is the core challenge — BMW Group Financial Services has internal EV RV desks.
Partners & Pain
BMW Group customers globally. ⚠️ BMW GFV programs are captive-only — BMW Group Financial Services bears the RV risk internally or reinsures via specialist RVI providers.
Scale
BMW Group global unit sales ~2.5M (2024). GFV penetration not publicly disclosed.
C3 Hyundai Capital (HMF / KMF) Captive / GFV
Korean OEM captive finance · GFV in Korea and global markets · Seoul · Korea · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Hyundai Motor Finance (HMF) and Kia Motors Finance (KMF) are the captive finance arms of Hyundai Motor Group. Operate GFV programs in Korea, US, Europe, and select Asian markets.
GFV-based PCP products in mature markets (US, UK, Germany, Australia). In India: no captive finance arm — Hyundai-branded finance products are distributed via third-party banks. GFV in India requires a captive structure.
Pivots
Expanding GFV and EV-linked finance products globally. EV-specific GFV products for IONIQ range. In India: exploring tripartite structures (as was tried in Hyundai-HDFC-Cars24 deal that dissolved).
Partners & Pain
Hyundai/Kia customers in mature markets. ⚠️ Hyundai India lacks a captive — this is why the HDFC-Hyundai-Cars24 tripartite structure was attempted but ultimately dissolved (too many parties).
Scale
Hyundai Motor Group global unit sales ~7M (2024). GFV penetration varies widely by market.
C4 Toyota Financial Services / Lexus Financial Services Captive / GFV
OEM captive · longest-running GFV in Japan and global · Tokyo · Japan · Global
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Toyota Financial Services (TFS) established 1982. One of the longest-running captive auto finance programs globally. Lexus Financial Services is the premium sub-brand.
GFV products in Japan (Toyota's home market), US, UK, Australia, and other major markets. Toyota Guaranteed Auto Protection (GAP). Lexus Financial Services: luxury GFV programs similar to Mercedes/BMW.
Pivots
Accelerating EV GFV products for bZ4X, Prius PHEV ranges. TFS managing EV RV risk internally — one of the largest EV RV exposures globally.
Partners & Pain
Toyota/Lexus customers globally. ⚠️ TFS manages EV RV risk internally — one of the largest RV risk holders in the world given Toyota's global scale.
Scale
Toyota global unit sales ~11M (2024). TFS total managed assets ~$200B+.
C5 Maruti Suzuki True Value / Skoda Superb / VinFast (India) Captive / GFV
India OEM RVI programs — internal reference cases · India
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Three India OEM reference cases for assured buyback. Maruti Suzuki True Value: buyback via certified pre-owned network. Skoda Superb: assured buyback at launch for premium segment. VinFast: aggressive assured buyback to overcome EV depreciation anxiety.
Maruti: True Value certified pre-owned network acts as guaranteed exit for existing customers. Skoda Superb: assured buyback at a guaranteed % of purchase price after 3 years — premium segment confidence. VinFast: 70% buyback guarantee after 3 years or equivalent — most aggressive India program.
Pivots
All 3 programs validated that assured buyback works in India when tied to new car launch (Skoda, VinFast) or structured as ecosystem loop (Maruti True Value). These are the primary India domestic reference cases.
Partners & Pain
New car buyers in India. ⚠️ These are internal / captive programs — not third-party RVI. The Lockton-Zuno-MG structure is the only third-party RVI reference in India.
Scale
India-domestic programs. VinFast India sales modest (~5,000 units 2024). Skoda Superb limited volumes. Maruti True Value: 500+ outlets, 400,000+ certified pre-owned units.
O · OEM Buyback India — Live Programs Live India-specific OEM buyback cases
O1 MG Motor India + Lockton + Zuno OEM Buyback India
Live third-party RVI in India — the primary reference case · India
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
MG Motor India launched India's first third-party structured RVI program — a tripartite between MG Motor (OEM), Lockton (structuring broker), and Zuno/Edelweiss (insurance capacity).
MG Assured Buyback: customer buys MG car with guaranteed buyback at 50–60% of purchase price after 3 years. The RVI risk is held by Zuno (Edelweiss General Insurance), structured by Lockton. OEM marketing the product without holding the risk — the India template for third-party RVI.
Pivots
Program launched ~2022–2023 as MG's EV lineup (ZS EV, Comet EV) required depreciation anxiety removal for EV customers. Model has been referenced by Skoda, Maruti, and VinFast as a template.
Partners & Pain
MG Motor India customers (primarily EV buyers). Lockton structures and places risk. Zuno/Edelweiss holds RVI capacity. ⚠️ This is the ONLY live third-party OEM RVI program in India — the Lockton-OEM-Zuno tripartite is the template Cars24/NBFC should study.
Scale
MG Motor India ~50,000 units/year. RVI premium volume and loss ratio not publicly disclosed.
O2 Nissan India (Exploring) OEM Buyback India
Actively being explored by Cars24 as first OEM EV buyback partnership · India
●●●●●
RVI scale
Established & Products
Nissan India exploring RVI / assured buyback for new EV launch confidence. Not yet contracted — actively in conversation with Cars24 Suraksha team.
Use case: OEM uses Cars24 Suraksha guaranteed buyback to eliminate customer depreciation anxiety at new EV launch window. Model: Cars24 provides the buyback guarantee (backed by Suraksha), OEM markets it as assured buyback at point of sale.
Pivots
Active exploration — not yet tried in this specific framing. Strong external precedent: MG (Lockton-Zuno), Skoda Superb, VinFast.
Partners & Pain
Nissan India (OEM partner). Cars24 Suraksha (buyback guarantor). Potential lender / RVI insurer (to be determined). ⚠️ Timing is critical — most effective at launch window when Cx is in purchase mode.
Scale
Nissan India unit sales ~15,000/year (2024). No buyback program live yet — exploratory stage only.
🧭 Philosophy & Moats
🎯 6 Strategic Themes
🛠️ Projects & KPIs
⚡ Execution Priorities
⭐ North Stars
PEOPLE & CULTURE · MISSION
Build a high-performance, AI-enabled, people-first organisation that drives business outcomes through talent excellence, workforce discipline, and continuous learning.
5 Moats · 6 Themes · 11 Projects · 6 KPIs · FY 2026–27

5 Strategic Moats

🎯
Performance Excellence
Structured goal alignment & merit-based differentiation
🤝
Talent Retention
Proactive attrition management & win-back programs
🤖
AI-First Workplace
AI-led recruitment, training & HR automation
🌐
Diversity & Inclusion
20%+ diversity representation across roles
⏱️
Workforce Discipline
>90% on-time attendance & governance

6 Strategic Themes — FY 2026–27

01 Performance Management
GAPP quarterly reviews + PACE annual cycle for structured, merit-based evaluation and career growth.
02 Talent Retention
Attrition management below 3% MoM with proactive win-back programs for high performers.
03 Employee Experience
PEP Centralisation for consistent engagement initiatives and structured feedback loops.
04 Diversity & Inclusion
Diversity hiring strategy targeting >20% representation across business and functional roles.
05 AI in Recruitment & L&D
HiREVV AI platform, resume parsing, AI-led NHT and refresher training at scale.
06 HR Transformation
Employee lifecycle automation covering onboarding, documentation and 50%+ manual HR process reduction.

STRATEGIC ROADMAP
NBFC Product & Technology Roadmap
AI First. Paperless. High Velocity. Seamless Customer Experience.
This roadmap outlines how we will structurally transform TAT, Growth, Risk, Cost, and Technology over the next six months. The strategy combines AI-led automation, distribution scale, operational control, architectural simplification, and international readiness.

Our Operating Philosophy

🤖
AI First by Default
Before adding human effort, we evaluate whether AI can automate, augment, or eliminate the step entirely.
🗄️
Data from Source, Not Documents
Move to end-to-end paperless journeys. Fetch verified data from AA, Digilocker, GST, EPFO and system integrations.
📈
Exponential Productivity Uplift
We will not invest in 10% productivity improvements. Every bet must aim for 2x–3x output per agent.
🎯
Double Down on Pre-Approvals
Top-ups, LAC, PL, and ecosystem integrations like CRED and PhonePe are core growth levers.
❤️
Customer Experience Beyond TAT
Customers repeat when the first experience is seamless, transparent, and predictable.
🛡️
Hygiene is Always P0
No PII in sheets. No broken instrumentation. No unmanaged tech debt.

Themes & North Star Metrics

These North Stars align operational improvements directly with measurable business outcomes
C2D TAT (Conversion to Disbursal)
NORTH STAR
● P30 C2D TAT < 5 minutes
● P70 C2D TAT < 1 hour
L2A TAT
NORTH STAR
● P80 TAT < 10 minutes
Growth
NORTH STAR
● Enablement for 100% disbursal growth across channels
Customer Experience
NORTH STAR
● 80% reduction in dealer grievances
● Customer NPS uplift from 47 to 70
Collections
NORTH STAR
● 100% automation of workflows
Cost Reduction
NORTH STAR
● 15+ lacs per month cost savings
Risk Reduction
NORTH STAR
● Eliminate open approval risk and fraud exposure
C2D TAT Transformation
Collapse Conversion to Disbursal Time through comprehensive automation, AI-powered decisioning, and end-to-end process integration.
● THEME: C2D TAT
C2D TAT: End-to-End Automation
BOF Fulfilment DIY
P0
Current State
All KYC and NACH fulfilment journeys are agent-driven, creating dependency, higher TAT, and operational costs.
Future State
100% fulfilment journeys completed without agent assistance through self-service digital interfaces.
Business Impact
Reduced TAT by 30-40%, lower operational costs through agent deflection, scalable infrastructure without linear headcount growth.
FCU Reimagine
P0
Current State
FCU reviews every case irrespective of data quality, creating bottlenecks and delays for verified source data cases.
Future State
50%+ cohort skips FCU using verified source data from Account Aggregator, Digilocker, EPFO, and GST APIs.
Business Impact
Faster approvals, significant FCU load reduction, consistent decisioning based on verified data, ability to scale without proportional FCU expansion.
● THEME: L2A TAT
L2A TAT Acceleration
AI Calling – TVR
P0
Current State
Credit agents manually conduct all reference and TVR calls with inconsistent quality and limited scalability.
Future State
95%+ calls automated via AI with natural language processing, automated fraud detection, and instant report generation.
Business Impact
Standardized call quality, scalable outreach without linear agent growth, detailed analytics on call outcomes, reduced L2A TAT.
Underwriting AI Copilot
P1
Current State
Agents manually interpret data from 5-6 sources, ~10 cases/day capacity with high cognitive load and inconsistency.
Future State
3x productivity with AI synthesis of all data sources, risk highlighting, decision recommendations, P80 TAT < 10 minutes.
Business Impact
Underwriting capacity 10→30 cases/agent/day without hiring, consistent quality, reduced training time, faster L2A TAT.
● THEME: GROWTH
Growth: Distribution Multipliers
Conversion Agent Copilot
P0
Current State
Agents pitch without data-driven recommendations or customer intelligence, resulting in inconsistent conversion rates.
Future State
AI-recommended pitch based on customer profile, credit history, affordability signals, and cohort behavior patterns.
Business Impact
10-15% conversion uplift, consistent pitch quality, better product-customer fit, reduced training time for new agents.
Productisation of RC Limit for Dealers
P0
Current State
Dealer RC limits tracked offline in spreadsheets with no systematic controls or automated enforcement.
Future State
Platform-level RC limit tracking with automated controls, real-time visibility, and breach alerts.
Business Impact
Reduced credit risk exposure, automated limit enforcement, better dealer credit management, audit-ready controls.
Live AI Bot in Sourcing Funnel
P0
Current State
25-30% customers drop off when they have questions, no real-time assistance available during application.
Future State
Native AI bot embedded in journey with instant FAQ responses, eligibility checks, document guidance, 24/7 availability.
Business Impact
20-25% drop-off reduction, improved customer experience, capture off-hours demand, lower agent dependency.
Trade Advance
P0
Current State
Manual pilot with 10 dealers, ops-heavy onboarding, no systematic playbook for scale.
Future State
100 dealers live with 2x lead generation per dealer through streamlined onboarding and self-service portal.
Business Impact
10x dealer scale, validated playbook for dealer-led growth, significant incremental disbursal volume.
PA Journey – Offer Enhancement
P0
Current State
Pre-approved offer system does not exist, missing opportunity for proactive high-quality customer targeting.
Future State
Strengthened pre-approved funnel with ML-driven targeting, multi-channel delivery, one-click acceptance.
Business Impact
New 60-70% conversion channel vs 20-25% organic, lower CAC, faster disbursals, competitive moat.
PA Journey – PhonePe
P0
Current State
Basic PhonePe integration with 15-18% drop-offs due to lack of personalization and context.
Future State
Targeted pre-approved flows with data pre-fill, optimized UX, intelligent nudges to 2x disbursals.
Business Impact
₹30-50 Cr incremental monthly disbursals, access to 450M+ PhonePe users, embedded finance advantage.
Internal Top-Up
P0
Current State
Top-up product does not exist, losing high-quality low-CAC growth from existing customers to competitors.
Future State
₹10 Cr monthly run rate through proactive offers based on payment history and credit profile.
Business Impact
High-margin zero-CAC channel, 70%+ conversion, improved LTV, reduced churn, foundation for cross-sell.
Elite Cars BRE Handling
P0
Current State
Elite car segment lacks proper BRE workflows, creating process inconsistency and handling.
Future State
Comprehensive BRE workflows for elite cars with specialized underwriting rules and risk controls.
Business Impact
Proper risk management for high-value segment, process standardization, improved portfolio quality.
CRED Product Flow
P1
Current State
CRED integration does not exist, missing access to high-quality credit-savvy customer segment.
Future State
100 loans per month through seamless CRED app integration with pre-filled applications and fast decisioning.
Business Impact
Access to premium customers, brand association, learning for fintech partnerships, potential 5x scale.
Organic Funnel Optimisation
P1
Current State
18-22% L2A conversion with friction-heavy journey, no pre-fills, multiple form fields, unclear errors.
Future State
30% L2A uplift through intelligent pre-fills, progressive disclosure, smart defaults, A/B tested UX.
Business Impact
Significant incremental monthly disbursals with same traffic, better marketing ROI, improved customer experience.
Refinance Policies on Sentinel
P1
Current State
Legacy decision science code makes policy experiments slow (3-4 week cycles), limiting competitive agility.
Future State
Business-configurable policy rules on Sentinel with A/B testing framework and real-time impact monitoring.
Business Impact
5-8% approval rate improvement through rapid experimentation, faster time-to-market, reduced engineering dependency.
● THEME: COLLECTIONS
Collections Infrastructure
NACH Presentation Automation
P0
Current State
Semi-automated file-based NACH process with manual reconciliation, delays in bank presentation, and limited real-time visibility.
Future State
100% automated NACH presentation with real-time API integration, automated validation, and instant status tracking.
Business Impact
Faster NACH registration, eliminated manual errors, improved success rates, complete compliance audit trail.
Settlement Workflow Enhancements
P0
Current State
Manual settlement marking between Collections Gateway and LMS requires ops intervention and reconciliation efforts.
Future State
Automated CG ↔ LMS integration for real-time settlement marking with automated reconciliation and exception handling.
Business Impact
Reduced settlement TAT, eliminated manual errors, real-time settlement visibility, automated accounting sync.
Legal Module
P0
Current State
Legal case tracking handled offline in spreadsheets with no systematic workflow or visibility into case status and outcomes.
Future State
End-to-end legal case management system with workflow automation, document management, and outcome tracking.
Business Impact
Complete legal case visibility, streamlined workflows, better recovery coordination, audit-ready documentation.
Document Flow to Collections
P0
Current State
Limited documents available to collections teams, forcing agents to request docs from multiple sources.
Future State
All loan documents automatically available in collections platform including agreements, KYC, payment history.
Business Impact
Improved collections effectiveness, reduced document search time, better customer conversations with full context.
Location Service Modernization
P0
Current State
Old architecture with frequent downtime, slow response times, and inability to scale during peak loads.
Future State
Cloud-native architecture with 99.9% uptime, sub-second response, horizontal scalability, comprehensive monitoring.
Business Impact
Higher service reliability, better customer experience, ability to handle growth, reduced maintenance overhead.
● THEME: COST REDUCTION
Cost Reduction
Bureau Cost Optimization
P0
Current State
CIBIL pulled at early booking stage creates redundant pulls for non-converting cases, multiple teams pull independently.
Future State
Strategic bureau pulls closer to disbursal intent with caching, shared pulls, and intelligent refresh decisions.
Business Impact
₹1 Cr annual savings, improved unit economics without compromising underwriting quality.
cKYC Automatic Updates
P0
Current State
Manual cKYC update process creates operational overhead and delays in customer onboarding.
Future State
Automated cKYC updates through system integration with CERSAI, eliminating manual intervention.
Business Impact
₹4 lacs per month cost savings, faster customer onboarding, reduced ops dependency, improved compliance.
Co-Lending Phase 2
P1
Current State
Manual data entry on Bajaj SFDC creates 15-20% sendback rate, delays, and partner relationship friction.
Future State
Zero reversals with 100% automated doc and data sharing via API integration and real-time sync.
Business Impact
Eliminated sendbacks, 80% ops overhead reduction, faster disbursals, scalable infrastructure for new partners.
● THEME: RISK REDUCTION
Risk Reduction
Dedupe
P0
Current State
Limited dedupe coverage (exact matches only) allows sophisticated fraud through name variations, family member applications.
Future State
100% dedupe coverage with fuzzy matching, device fingerprinting, family linking, ML-based fraud ring detection.
Business Impact
Significant fraud prevention, improved portfolio quality, regulatory compliance, competitive advantage in fraud controls.
MapMyIndia Integration
P0
Current State
Coarse serviceability controls create either excessive risk exposure or missed business opportunities.
Future State
Granular pin-code level serviceability rules with risk-based approval controls and automated verification.
Business Impact
Reduced geographic risk exposure, optimized market coverage, data-driven serviceability expansion decisions.
Deviation & Delegation Authority
P2
Current State
Offline deviation approval logic creates process opacity and compliance risk.
Future State
System-enforced deviation rules with automated delegation authority and complete audit trails.
Business Impact
Process transparency, automated controls, audit readiness, consistent policy enforcement across all cases.
● THEME: CUSTOMER & DEALER EXP
Customer & Dealer Experience
Agent & Manager Control Tower
P0
Current State
No centralized operational visibility, PII floating in sheets, teams operating in silos with separate dashboards.
Future State
Centralized control tower with real-time ops metrics, SLA tracking, zero PII in sheets, automated alerting.
Business Impact
Audit readiness, proactive compliance monitoring, executive visibility, PII risk elimination, foundation for ISO/SOC certs.
Automated Communications
P0
Current State
Manual communication processes for VAS, delivery confirmation, NPS surveys, and OVD with inconsistent execution.
Future State
Standardized automated messaging with template management, triggered workflows, and comprehensive tracking.
Business Impact
Consistent customer touchpoints, reduced manual effort, improved response rates, better customer engagement analytics.
Payment Refund Flow Automation
P0
Current State
Manual refund processing with unclear communication to customers and no bulk processing capability.
Future State
Automated refund workflow with clear customer communication, bulk processing, and real-time status tracking.
Business Impact
Faster customer refunds, improved satisfaction, reduced ops overhead, better cash flow management, audit trail.
Dealer & Sales Copilot
P0
Current State
RMs handle 30-40 repetitive dealer queries daily (commission, case status, grievances), 5-10 mins each.
Future State
AI copilot provides instant answers with self-service portal and 24/7 support capability.
Business Impact
40% RM time freed for sales activities, instant dealer query resolution, improved satisfaction, scalable support.
Loan Cancellations
P0
Current State
Cancellations handled outside system with manual accounting reversals, delayed refunds, and no analytics on cancellation patterns.
Future State
Fully automated cancellation workflow with in-system processing, automated accounting sync, and refund automation.
Business Impact
Faster customer refunds (24 hours vs 3-5 days), reduced errors, complete audit trail, insights into cancellation drivers.
Commission Visibility
P0
Current State
Dealers lack transparency into commission calculations and payout timelines, creating disputes and relationship friction.
Future State
Real-time commission visibility with case-level calculation transparency, payout schedules, and automated notifications.
Business Impact
Improved Dealer NPS, 100% reduction in commission disputes, faster resolution, better dealer retention.
Holdback Release Automation
P0
Current State
Manual holdback release with 5-7 day TAT, manual verification steps, and poor dealer working capital management.
Future State
80%+ holdbacks released within 24 hours through automated verification, rule-based approvals, and straight-through processing.
Business Impact
Improved dealer cash flow, better dealer satisfaction, significant ops savings, scalable for growth.
Post Disbursal Journey
P1
Current State
No structured post-disbursal experience, customers contact support for basic queries (~24 hour resolution).
Future State
Comprehensive self-service portal with payment schedules, statements, foreclosure calculator, NOC tracking, AI chatbot.
Business Impact
Significant support ticket reduction, improved customer satisfaction, cross-sell foundation, scalable customer experience.

AI Adoption & Engineering Standardization

Standardization of Daily AI Tool Usage Tracking
Establish unified dashboard to track AI adoption rates, productivity uplift, code quality metrics, and identify potential pitfalls across engineering teams.
Cursor
FE Engineers
Move to Codex by early March
Windsurf
BE Engineers
Move to Claude by end March
ChatGPT
All Teams
Standardize usage patterns
Claude
BE Engineers
Primary BE tool
Codex
FE Engineers
Primary FE tool
Macroscope
All Engineers
Code review assistance
Transition Plan & Objectives
Frontend Engineers
Migrate from Cursor to Codex (100% completion by early March)
Backend Engineers
Migrate from Windsurf to Claude (100% completion by end March)
Code Review
Macroscope assists in reviewing all engineer-written code for quality and consistency
Unified Dashboard Metrics
Track & Measure
● AI adoption rates across teams
● Productivity uplift per engineer
● Code quality and bug reduction metrics
● Pitfalls and risk area identification
Leadership Visibility
Empower leadership to monitor AI-driven productivity transformation, make data-driven decisions on tool investments, and identify opportunities for further automation and efficiency gains.
